zh: 兄弟之间的分歧不能切断他们的血缘关系。
en: Differences between brothers cannot sever their blood ties.

zh: 我们就像海中的岛屿，表面分离，深处相连。
en: We are like islands in the sea, separate on the surface but connected in the deep.

zh: 在所有可以想象的方式中，家庭是我们与过去的纽带，也是通往未来的桥梁。
en: In every conceivable manner, the family is link to our past, bridge to our future.

zh: 我们呼吁各国人民共同努力，构建人类命运共同体，建设一个开放、包容、清洁、美丽的世界，享受持久和平、普遍安全和共同繁荣。
en: We call on the people of all countries to work together, to build a community with a shared future for mankind, to build an open, inclusive, clean, and beautiful world that enjoys lasting peace, universal security, and common prosperity.

zh: 研究表明，普通人通常只与七到十五个人保持定期沟通，事实上我们的大部分沟通是与最亲近的五到十个人进行的。
en: Research shows the average person only has regular communication with between seven and fifteen people, and that most of our communication is in fact with five to ten people who are closest to us.

zh: 然而，也许我们与世界其他地区的联系比我们想象的要更紧密。
en: However, perhaps we are closer to the rest of the world than we think.

zh: “六度分隔”理论指的是，地球上任何一个人都可以通过不超过五个人的链条与任何其他人联系起来。
en: "Six Degrees of Separation" refers to the theory that any person on Earth can be connected to any other person through a chain of no more than five other people.

zh: 这个概念最早在20世纪20年代就被讨论过。
en: The concept was first talked about as long ago as in the 1920s.

zh: 匈牙利作家弗里杰什·卡林西于1929年出版了一本名为《一切皆不同》的书，他在书中介绍了友谊网络的概念，他的想法影响了我们早期对社交网络的许多印象。
en: The Hungarian author Frigyes Karinthy published a book called Everything Is Different in 1929, in which he introduced the idea of friendship networks and his ideas influenced many of our early impressions of social networks.

zh: 在20世纪50年代，两位科学家尝试用数学方法证明这一理论；但二十年后，他们仍未取得任何成功。
en: In the 1950s, an attempt was made by two scientists to prove the theory mathematically; but after twenty years, they still had not had any success.

zh: 1967年，一位名叫斯坦利·米尔格拉姆的美国社会学家尝试用一种新方法来检验这一理论。
en: In 1967, an American sociologist called Stanley Milgram tried using a new method to test the theory.

zh: 他随机选择了美国中部的一些人，要求他们将包裹寄给马萨诸塞州的一个陌生人。
en: He chose a random sample of people in the middle of America and asked them to send packages to a stranger in the state of Massachusetts.

zh: 寄送包裹的人只知道陌生人的姓名、职业和大致位置。
en: The people sending the packages only knew the name, job and general location of the stranger.

zh: 米尔格拉姆告诉他们，将包裹寄给他们个人认识、并且认为可能认识目标陌生人的人。
en: Milgram told them to send the package to a person they knew personally who they thought might know the target stranger.

zh: 一旦这个人收到了包裹，他/她就会将包裹转寄给他们的一个联系人，直到包裹能被亲自送达正确的人手中。
en: Once the parcel had been received by this person, he / she would send the parcel onto a contact of theirs until the parcel could be personally delivered to the correct person.

zh: 令人惊讶的是，只需要五到七个人就能将包裹送达，结果一经发布，就被刊登在双月刊杂志《今日心理学》上。
en: Amazingly, it only took between five and seven people to get the parcels delivered, and once released, the results were published in the bimonthly magazine Psychology Today.

zh: 正是这项研究启发了‘六度分隔’这个说法。
en: It was this research that inspired the phrase "Six Degrees of Separation".

zh: 在过去的几十年里，这个理论和说法再次出现。
en: In the last few decades, the theory and the phrase have appeared again.

zh: 它的名字被用作一部戏剧的标题，后来又成了一部电影。
en: Its name was used as the title of a play and then a film.

zh: 随后，更多基于这一概念的电影和电视节目被制作并播出。
en: Then, more films and TV programmes based on the concept were made and broadcast.

zh: 例如，获得奥斯卡奖的电影《通天塔》就是基于‘六度分隔’的概念。
en: For example, the Oscar-winning film Babel is based on the concept of "Six Degrees of Separation".

zh: 所有角色的生活都紧密相连，尽管他们彼此不认识，并且生活在数千英里之外。
en: The lives of all the characters were closely connected, although they did not know each other and lived thousands of miles apart.

zh: 电视剧《迷失》也探讨了‘六度分隔’的概念，因为几乎所有的角色都曾随机相遇过，或者遇到过其他角色认识的人，然后他们才都遭遇了同一场空难。
en: The television series Lost also explored the idea of "Six Degrees of Separation", as almost all the characters had randomly met each other, or had met someone the other characters knew, before they were all in the same plane crash.

zh: 在20世纪90年代中期，美国的两名大学生发明了一个游戏。
en: In the mid-1990s, two college students in the United States invented a game.

zh: 这个游戏的想法是通过不超过六个链接，将任何演员与著名的美国演员兼音乐家凯文·贝肯联系起来。
en: The idea of the game was to link any actor to Kevin Bacon, a famous American actor and musician, through no more than six links.

zh: 很快，这个游戏就在美国各地的大学里流行起来。
en: Soon the game was being played in universities across the United States.

zh: 2003年，哥伦比亚大学试图在互联网上重现米尔格拉姆的实验。
en: In 2003, Columbia University tried to recreate Milgram's experiment on the Internet.

zh: 这被称为‘哥伦比亚小世界项目’。
en: This became known as the "Columbia Small-world Project".

zh: 该实验涉及24,163条电子邮件链，目标人物是13个不同国家的18个人。
en: The experiment involved 24,163 email chains with 18 target people in 13 different countries.

zh: 结果证实，链中的平均链接数为六。
en: The results confirmed that the average number of links in the chain was six.

zh: 最近，2011年米兰大学的一项实验分析了7.21亿社交媒体用户之间的关系，发现92%的人仅通过四个阶段，或者说五度分隔，就能联系起来。
en: Most recently, an experiment in 2011 at the University of Milan analysed the relationship between 721 million social media users and found that 92 percent were connected by only four stages, or five degrees of separation.

zh: 所以，想一想：你可能会如何与你经常乘坐的公交车司机或为你送包裹的人产生联系？
en: So, think about it for a minute: How might you be connected to the driver of the bus you regularly take or the person who delivers your parcels?

zh: 这个概念早在20世纪20年代就被首次讨论。
en: The concept was first talked about as long ago as in the 1920s.

zh: 在20世纪50年代，两位科学家尝试用数学方法证明这个理论。
en: In the 1950s, an attempt was made by two scientists to prove the theory mathematically.

zh: 一旦这个人收到了包裹，他/她会将包裹发送给他们的一个联系人，直到包裹可以亲自送达正确的人手中。
en: Once the parcel had been received by this person, he / she would send the parcel onto a contact of theirs until the parcel could be personally delivered to the correct person.

zh: 它的名字被用作一部戏剧的标题，然后是一部电影。
en: Its name was used as the title of a play and then a film.

zh: 很快，这个游戏就在美国各地的大学里流行起来。
en: Soon the game was being played in universities across the United States.

zh: 《绿山墙的安妮》是加拿大作家露西·莫德·蒙哥马利的一部小说，出版于1908年。
en: Anne of Green Gables is a novel by Canadian writer Lucy Maud Montgomery, and was published in 1908.

zh: 故事发生在20世纪初加拿大的爱德华王子岛。
en: The story is set on Canada's Prince Edward Island early in the 20th century.

zh: 兄妹马修和玛丽拉住在一个叫做“绿山墙”的农场上。
en: A brother and sister, Matthew and Marilla live on a farm called "Green Gables".

zh: 他们申请从孤儿院领养一个男孩，因为他们的农场需要帮手，但相反，他们被介绍了一个名叫安妮的女孩。
en: They apply to adopt a boy from an orphanage as they need help on their farm, but instead they are presented with a girl named Anne.

zh: 在安妮到达的那天，马修去火车站接她。
en: On the day Anne is to arrive, Matthew picks her up at the train station.

zh: 玛丽拉看见马修在前院，立刻冲到门口。
en: Marilla saw Matthew in the front yard and immediately rushed to the door.

zh: 但当她的目光落在这个穿着僵硬丑陋裙子、留着长长红发、有着热切明亮眼睛的奇怪小身影上时，她惊讶地愣住了。
en: But when her eyes fell on the odd little figure in the stiff, ugly dress, with the long red hair and the eager, bright eyes, she froze in amazement.

zh: “马修·卡斯伯特，那是谁？”她问道。
en: "Matthew Cuthbert, who's that?" she asked.

zh: “没有什么男孩，”马修说。
en: "There wasn't any boy," said Matthew.

zh: 他朝那孩子点了点头，想起他甚至从未问过她的名字。
en: He nodded at the child, remembering that he had never even asked her name.

zh: “没有男孩！但肯定应该有个男孩的，”玛丽拉坚持说。
en: "No boy! But there must have been a boy," insisted Marilla.

zh: “我们捎信给斯宾塞太太让她带个男孩来。”
en: "We sent word to Mrs Spencer to bring a boy."

zh: “嗯，她没有。她带了她来。她到了火车站，不能把她一个人留在那里。”
en: "Well, she didn't. She brought her. She arrived at the train station and couldn't be left there alone."

zh: 在这段对话期间，孩子一直保持沉默。
en: During this dialogue the child had remained silent.

zh: 突然，她似乎完全理解了刚才所说的话的含义。
en: Suddenly she seemed to grasp the full meaning of what had been said.

zh: 她向前跳了一步，双手紧握。
en: She sprang forward a step and clasped her hands.

zh: “你们不想要我！”女孩哭喊道。
en: "You don't want me!" the girl cried.

zh: “你们不想要我是因为我不是一个男孩！
en: "You don't want me because I'm not a boy!

zh: 我早该料到会这样。
en: I might have expected it.

zh: 我早该知道这一切太美好了，难以持久。
en: I might have known it was all too beautiful to last.

zh: 我早该知道没有人真的想要我。
en: I might have known nobody really did want me.

zh: 哦，我该怎么办？
en: Oh, what am I going to do?

zh: 我要放声大哭了！”
en: I'm going to burst into tears!"

zh: 她果然放声大哭起来。
en: Burst into tears she did.

zh: 她在桌边的椅子上坐下，把胳膊扔在桌子上，把脸埋在里面，开始猛烈地哭起来。
en: Sitting down on a chair by the table, throwing her arms on it, and burying her face in them, she proceeded to cry stormily.

zh: 玛丽拉和马修面面相觑。
en: Marilla and Matthew looked at each other.

zh: 他们俩都不知道该说什么或做什么。
en: Neither of them knew what to say or do.

zh: 最后玛丽拉走上前去试图安慰这个孩子。
en: Finally Marilla stepped in to try to comfort the child.

zh: “好了，好了，没必要为这事哭得这么厉害。”
en: "Well, well, there's no need to cry so about it."

zh: “不，有必要！”孩子抬起头，露出一张泪流满面的脸。
en: "Yes, there is need!" The child raised her head, revealing a tear-stained face.

zh: “如果你是个孤儿，来到了一个你以为会成为家的地方，却发现他们因为你不是男孩而不想要你，你也会哭的。”
en: "You would cry, too, if you were an orphan and had come to a place you thought was going to be home and found that they didn't want you because you weren't a boy."

zh: “好了，别再哭了。我们今晚不会送你走。你得留在这里，直到我们调查完这件事。你叫什么名字？”
en: "Well, don't cry anymore. We're not going to send you off tonight. You'll have to stay here until we investigate this affair. What's your name?"

zh: “安妮，”孩子伤心地说。
en: "Anne," said the child sadly.

zh: “好了，来吧，安妮。该吃晚饭了。”
en: "Well, come along, Anne. It's dinner time."

zh: 他们都坐下来吃晚饭，但安妮吃不下。
en: They all sat down for dinner but Anne could not eat.

zh: 她试图享用盘子旁小玻璃碟里的面包、黄油和苹果酱，但她没有胃口。
en: She tried to enjoy the bread and butter and the apple jam out of the little glass dish by her plate but she had no appetite.

zh: “你什么都没吃，”玛丽拉尖锐地说，盯着她，好像这是个严重的问题。
en: "You're not eating anything," said Marilla sharply, eying her as if it were a serious problem.

zh: “我吃不下。我正处于绝望的深渊。当你处于绝望的深渊时，你能吃得下吗？”
en: "I can't. I'm in the depths of despair. Can you eat when you are in the depths of despair?"

zh: “我从未处于绝望的深渊，所以我说不好，”玛丽拉回答。
en: "I've never been in the depths of despair, so I can't say," responded Marilla.

zh: “你没有过吗？那么，你有没有试过想象自己处于绝望的深渊？”
en: "Weren't you? Well, did you ever try to imagine you were in the depths of despair?"

zh: “我猜她是累了，”马修说。“最好让她上床睡觉，玛丽拉。”
en: "I guess she's tired," said Matthew. "Best put her to bed, Marilla."

zh: 玛丽拉一直在想应该让安妮睡在哪里。
en: Marilla had been wondering where Anne should be put to bed.

zh: 她决定让安妮睡在一楼的小卧室。
en: She decided on the small bedroom on the first floor.

zh: 她点燃一支蜡烛，告诉安妮跟着她，安妮照做了，经过门厅桌子时拿上了她的帽子和包。
en: She lit a candle and told Anne to follow her, which Anne did, taking her hat and bag from the hall table as she passed.

zh: 门厅非常干净；她发现自己所在的小房间似乎更加干净。
en: The hall was perfectly clean; the little room in which she found herself seemed still cleaner.

zh: 玛丽拉把蜡烛放在一张三条腿的桌子上，掀开了床罩。
en: Marilla set the candle on a three-legged table and turned down the bedclothes.

zh: “好了，尽快脱衣服上床睡觉。我几分钟后回来拿蜡烛。我不敢让你自己熄灭它。你很可能把这里点着。”
en: "Well, undress as quick as you can and go to bed. I'll come back in a few minutes for the candle. I daren't trust you to put it out yourself. You'd likely set the place on fire."

zh: 玛丽拉走后，安妮伤心地环顾四周。
en: When Marilla had gone, Anne looked around her sadly.

zh: 粉刷过的墙壁光秃秃的，令人难受。
en: The whitewashed walls were so painfully bare.

zh: 地板也是光秃秃的。
en: The floor was bare, too.

zh: 一个角落里是床，一张高高的、老式的深色木床。
en: In one corner was the bed, a high, old-fashioned one of dark wood.

zh: 桌子和床中间是窗户，上面挂着一幅冰冷的白色窗帘。
en: Midway between table and bed was the window, with an icy white curtain over it.

zh: 没有卫生间，但另一个角落里有一个带水龙头的洗脸架。
en: There was no restroom, but there was a wash stand with a faucet in the other corner.

zh: 整个房间感觉冰冷而不受欢迎，这让安妮不寒而栗。
en: The whole room felt cold and unwelcoming, which sent a shiver through Anne's bones.

zh: 她抽泣着迅速脱掉衣服，穿上睡衣，跳上床，把脸埋进枕头里，用被子蒙住了头。
en: With a sob she quickly undressed, put on her nightclothes and jumped into bed where she pressed her face down into the pillow and pulled the clothes over her head.

zh: 当玛丽拉上来拿灯时，她看到衣服被乱七八糟地扔在地板上。
en: When Marilla came up for the light, she saw the untidy way the clothing had been thrown on the floor.

zh: 她小心地拾起安妮的衣服，整齐地放在一把黄色的椅子上，然后拿起蜡烛，走到床边。
en: She carefully picked up Anne's clothes, placed them neatly on a yellow chair, and then, taking up the candle, went over to the bed.

zh: “晚安，”她有点尴尬地说，但并非不友善。
en: "Good night," she said, a little awkwardly, but not unkindly.

zh: 安妮苍白的脸和大眼睛从被子上方露出来。
en: Anne's white face and big eyes appeared over the bedclothes.

zh: “你知道这一定是我度过的最糟糕的夜晚，你怎么能说晚安呢？”她不以为然地说。
en: "How can you call it a good night when you know it must be the very worst night I've ever had?" she said disapprovingly.

zh: 然后她又钻回了被子里。
en: Then she dived down into the bedclothes again.

zh: 玛丽拉收拾好碗碟后也上床睡觉了，眉头紧锁，非常坚决。
en: And to bed, when she had put her dishes away, went Marilla, frowning most resolutely.

zh: 而在楼上，在东边的山墙房间里，一个孤独、渴望关爱、没有朋友的孩子哭着睡着了。
en: And up-stairs, in the east gable, a lonely, heart-hungry, friendless child cried herself to sleep.

zh: 在美式英语和英式英语中，人们对相同的事物使用不同的表达方式。
en: People use different expressions for the same things in American English and British English.

zh: 例如，房子外面的空间在英式英语中被称为‘garden’，但在美式英语中被称为‘yard’。
en: For example, the space around the outside of a house is called a 'garden' in British English but a 'yard' in American English.

zh: 在英式英语中，他们谈论房子的‘front’或‘back’，而在美式英语中，他们说‘out back’或‘out front’。
en: In British English they talk about the 'front' or 'back' of a house, while in American English they say 'out back' or 'out front'.

zh: 最后，有时相同的词可以有不同的用法，例如，英式英语中的‘ground floor’在美式英语中被称为‘first floor’。
en: Finally, sometimes the same words can be used differently, for example, the 'ground floor' in British English is called the 'first floor' in American English.

zh: 去年我和家人去度假，拜访了一些我以前从未见过的远房亲戚。
en: My family and I went on a holiday last year to visit some distant relatives I had never met before.

zh: 他们住在纽约市中心的一间公寓里。
en: They lived in a flat in New York in the city centre.

zh: 拜访他们很令人兴奋，他们非常友好和热情！
en: It was exciting to visit them and they were very friendly and welcoming!

zh: 他们带我们去观光，我们乘坐了地铁，因为这是最快的出行方式。
en: They took us sightseeing and we went on the underground as it is the fastest way to get around.

zh: 我唯一的问题是在晚上睡觉。
en: The one problem I had was sleeping at night.

zh: 他们住在大楼的底层，所以我能听到城市的所有声音。
en: They live on the ground floor of their building so I could hear all the sounds of the city.

zh: 在夜间，卡车来收集垃圾，它们让我保持清醒。
en: During the night, lorries came to collect rubbish and they kept me awake.

zh: 他们明年要来伦敦拜访我们，我迫不及待地想带他们四处看看。
en: They are going to visit us in London next year and I can't wait to show them around.

zh: 街头节日建立社区精神
en: Street Festival Builds Community Spirit

zh: 9月21日，我们城市的首届社区街头节日在红木街举行。
en: On 21 September, the first Community Street Festival in our city was held on Redwood Street.

zh: 这个节日非常成功，吸引了来自20多个社区的超过10,000人。
en: The festival was highly successful, attracting over 10,000 people from over 20 communities.

zh: 天气很好，尽管早上下了大雨。
en: The weather was good, although it rained heavily in the morning.

zh: 表演者相当出色，并受到了公众的热烈欢迎。
en: The performers were rather good and well received by the public.

zh: 参加活动的人主要是家庭。
en: People attending the event were mainly families.

zh: 有各个年龄段的人。
en: There were people of all ages.

zh: 有各种各样的表演，其中大部分是唱歌和跳舞。
en: There were a wide variety of acts, most of which were singing and dancing.

zh: 吸引儿童的表演特别受欢迎。
en: The acts appealing to children were particularly popular.

zh: 节日的亮点是一群孩子的音乐和舞蹈。
en: The highlight of the festival was the music and dance by a group of children.

zh: 所有的表演者都准时开始和结束了他们的表演。
en: All of the performers started and finished their acts on time.

zh: 观众们很享受这些表演。
en: The audience enjoyed the performances.

zh: 还有许多供应食品和饮料的摊位。
en: There were also many outlets serving food and drink.

zh: 这个节日给许多人带来了巨大的快乐，并将我们城市内不同的社区聚集在一起。
en: The festival gave great pleasure to a large number of people and it brought together the different communities within our city.

zh: 一些人说：“我们渴望参加明年的节日。”
en: Some people said, "We are eager to participate in next year's festival."

zh: 你会根据他人的行为来判断他们吗？
en: Do you judge others by their behaviour?

zh: 本视频对基于人们行为形成和更新印象进行了分析。
en: This video presents an analysis of forming and updating impressions based on people's behaviour.

zh: 想象你去看了一场足球比赛。
en: Imagine you went to see a football game.

zh: 坐在你旁边的男人很吵闹，把饮料洒在你身上，并且取笑你的球队。
en: The man sitting next to you was loud, spilt his drink on you, and made fun of your team.

zh: 几天后，你在公园里散步，这时开始下雨了。
en: Days later, you're walking in the park when it starts to rain.

zh: 足球比赛中的同一个男人出现在你身边，给你提供了一把雨伞。
en: The same man from the football game shows up at your side to offer you an umbrella.

zh: 我们会很快根据他人的行为形成持久的第一印象。
en: We're quick to form lasting first impressions of others based on their behaviours.

zh: 我们几乎不费力气，仅凭单一行为就能形成对他人的印象。
en: We form impressions of others with little effort and from a single behaviour.

zh: 我们不能根据新的信息改变我们的印象。
en: We cannot change our impressions in light of new information.

zh: 非常负面、高度不道德的信息比非常正面、高度道德的信息具有。
en: Very negative, highly immoral information has a than very positive, highly moral information.

zh: 当了解另一个人的能力和才干时，会被更重地权衡。
en: When learning about another person's abilities and competences, the gets weighed more heavily.

zh: 高度不道德的行为和高度有才干的行为是人们倾向于权衡的行为。
en: Highly immoral actions and highly competent actions are behaviours that people tend to weigh.

zh: 大脑决定该行为是典型的还是，以便做出复杂的决定。
en: The brain decides whether the behaviour is typical or in order to make complex decisions.

zh: 更关心非常负面、不道德的事情是由于那些不良行为的相对稀有性。
en: Caring more about the very negative, immoral things is of the comparative rarity of those bad behaviour.

zh: “坏”可能比“好”更强大，仅仅因为“好”是。
en: "Bad" might be stronger than "good", only because "good" is.

zh: 根据演讲者的说法，在形成或更新对一个人的印象时，人们通常不是简单地通过判断他/她是否做了负面或不道德的事情来做决定，而是通过该行为是否相对较少发生。
en: According to the speaker, in forming or updating impressions of a person, people often make their decisions not simply by judging whether he / she has done something negative or immoral, but by whether that behaviour is comparatively less frequent.

zh: 每年有成千上万的游客涌入中国。
en: Every year thousands of tourists flock to China.

zh: 他们来参观长城和紫禁城的壮丽景色，但往往是胡同给人留下最深刻的印象，因为它们为旅行者提供了一个了解北京过去的罕见视角。
en: They come to see the grand sights of the Great Wall and the Forbidden City, but often it is the hutongs that leave the strongest impression, as they offer travellers a rare view into Beijing's past.

zh: 胡同——连接传统房屋矩形庭院的许多小巷——是中国古代建筑的一个特色。
en: Hutongs — the many little alleys that connect the rectangular courtyards of traditional houses — are a feature of ancient Chinese architecture.

zh: 然而，北京的胡同尤其著名，因为这里有成千上万的胡同。
en: Beijing's hutongs are particularly famous, however, as there are thousands of them.

zh: 如今，“胡同”一词的含义已不仅仅是连接庭院的巷子。
en: Nowadays, the word "hutong" has come to mean more than just the alleys that connect the courtyards.

zh: 它也指庭院本身，甚至指居住在那里的社区。
en: It also refers to the courtyards themselves and even to the communities that live there.

zh: 北京的大部分胡同建于13至19世纪的元、明、清时期。
en: The majority of Beijing's hutongs were built between the 13 and 19 centuries during the Yuan, Ming and Qing Dynasties.

zh: 为了帮助他们控制城市，这些时期的皇帝将北京的不同区域规划成围绕庭院建造的整齐的房屋街区。
en: To help them keep control over the city, the Emperors during these periods arranged different areas of Beijing in neat blocks of houses built around courtyards.

zh: 然后他们能够在各个胡同的入口处布置卫兵，这使得监视人们的行动变得更加容易。
en: They were then able to place guards at the entrances of the various hutongs, which made it easier to keep an eye on people's movements.

zh: 皇帝的家——紫禁城——位于北京的中心，贵族和富裕公民的家则位于最靠近皇宫的胡同里。
en: The Emperor's home, the Forbidden City, was in the centre of Beijing and the homes of the nobles and wealthy citizens were in the hutongs closest to the royal palace.

zh: 普通市民住在离宫殿更远的胡同里。
en: Ordinary citizens lived in the hutongs further away from the palace.

zh: 通过连接人们的家园，胡同实际上连接了人们的生活，无论是富人的生活还是普通市民的生活。
en: By connecting people's homes, the hutongs in fact connected people's lives, whether the lives of the rich or the lives of the ordinary citizens.

zh: 因为房屋是围绕庭院面对面建造的，所以居住在那里的家庭是彼此生活中重要的一部分。
en: Because the houses were built facing each other around courtyards, the families who lived there were an important part of each other's lives.

zh: 他们在需要帮助时互相支持，分享日常生活的快乐与悲伤，无疑还会一起分享食谱、借用拖把、焚烧香烛。
en: They supported each other when help was needed and shared the joy and sorrow of everyday life, no doubt sharing recipes, borrowing mops, and burning fragrant incense together.

zh: 因为胡同，庭院被连接在一起，绵延数英里，形成了一个人们工作、玩耍和生活在一起的网络——一个真正的社区。
en: Because of the hutongs, courtyards were joined together for miles around creating a network of people working, playing and living together — a real community.

zh: 到了清朝末期，随着政治形势给中国经济蒙上了一层阴影，北京胡同的条件下降了。
en: Towards the end of the Qing Dynasty, the conditions in Beijing's hutongs went down as the political situation cast a dark cloud on China's economy.

zh: 为了容纳不断增长的人口，许多新的胡同被迅速建造起来，但这些胡同建造得很差。
en: Many new hutongs were quickly built to house the increasing population but these were poorly made.

zh: 转折点出现在中华人民共和国成立时。
en: The turning point came when the People's Republic of China was set up.

zh: 条件得到了很大改善，政府承担了许多最古老胡同的保护工作。
en: Conditions were improved a great deal and the government undertook the preservation of many of the oldest hutongs.

zh: 胡同仍然是北京生活的重要组成部分，游客喜爱胡同也就不足为奇了。
en: Hutongs are still an important part of Beijing's life and it is not surprising that tourists love the hutongs.

zh: 他们可以漫步于有900年历史的三庙街，闲逛长达3公里的最长胡同东交民巷，或者挤过仅有40厘米宽的最窄胡同钱市胡同！
en: They can walk up Sanmiao Street, which dates back 900 years, wander down Dongjiaominxiang — the longest hutong at 3km, or squeeze through Qianshi — the narrowest at only 40cm wide!

zh: 胡同不仅连接了北京的街道和社区，还连接了它的过去和现在，表明北京确实是一座古老而又现代的城市。
en: The hutongs not only link Beijing's streets and communities, but also its past and present, showing that Beijing is truly an ancient yet modern city.

zh: 《威尼斯商人》是16世纪由世界著名的英国剧作家威廉·莎士比亚创作的戏剧。
en: The Merchant of Venice is a 16-century play written by the world-famous English playwright William Shakespeare.

zh: 剧中，巴萨尼奥是一位年轻贵族，他向犹太放债人夏洛克借钱，以便前往贝尔蒙特，希望能赢得富有的女继承人波西亚的芳心。
en: In the play, Bassanio is a young nobleman who borrows money from Shylock, a Jewish moneylender, so he can travel to Belmont in the hope of winning the hand of Portia, a rich heiress.

zh: 作为贷款的回报，巴萨尼奥的密友安东尼奥同意，如果巴萨尼奥无法偿还贷款，他将放弃“一磅肉”。
en: In return for the loan, Bassanio's close friend Antonio agrees to give up a "pound of flesh" if Bassanio can't repay the loan.

zh: 巴萨尼奥成功追求到波西亚，他们同意结婚，但随后安东尼奥收到消息，他的船在海上失事，他不可能给夏洛克一磅肉了。
en: Bassanio is successful in wooing Portia and they agree to marry, but then Antonio receives news that his ships have been lost at sea and it will be impossible for him to give Shylock a pound of his flesh.

zh: 当夏洛克发现这一点后，他开始对安东尼奥进行报复。
en: When Shylock finds out about this, he sets out to get his revenge on Antonio.

zh: 让宽恕见鬼去吧！我要正义。
en: To hell with forgiveness! I want justice.

zh: 协议必须完全履行。
en: The agreement must be completely honoured.

zh: 安东尼奥不能偿还这笔钱吗？
en: Is Antonio not able to repay the money?

zh: 是的，我可以向法庭提供安东尼奥债务两倍的钱。
en: Yes, I can offer the court twice the amount of Antonio's debt.

zh: 如果那还不够，我可以支付十倍的金额。
en: If that is not enough, I can pay ten times the amount.

zh: 如果连这都不够，那似乎复仇比正义更重要。
en: If even this is not enough, it would seem that revenge is more important than justice.

zh: 夏洛克，有人向你提供了三倍于贷款的钱。
en: Shylock, you have been offered three times the loan.

zh: 被告已经做出了承诺！我要违背这样的承诺吗？
en: A promise has already been made by the defendant! Shall I break such a promise?

zh: 确实债务尚未偿还，根据法律，你可以要求从安东尼奥心脏附近取一磅肉。
en: It is true that the debt has not been paid, and lawfully you can ask for a pound of flesh from near Antonio's heart.

zh: 接受三倍的钱吧。
en: Accept three times the money.

zh: 你似乎是一位明智的法官。
en: You seem to be a wise judge.

zh: 根据法律做出你的判决。
en: Make your judgment according to the law.

zh: 任何人的口舌之力都无法让我改变主意。
en: No power in the tongues of men will make me change my mind.

zh: 协议必须得到履行。
en: The agreement must be honoured.

zh: 准备好你的胸膛迎接他的刀，安东尼奥。
en: Prepare your chest for his knife, Antonio.

zh: 哦，明智的法官！哦，优秀的年轻人！
en: Oh, wise judge! Oh, excellent young man!

zh: 敞开你衬衫的前襟。
en: Open the front of your shirt.

zh: 是的，让我看看你的胸膛。
en: Yes, show me your chest.

zh: 这是在协议里的，不是吗，好法官？
en: It is in the agreement, is it not, good judge?

zh: “最靠近他的心脏”——这是原话。
en: "Nearest his heart"— those are the exact words.

zh: 你有称肉的秤吗？
en: Do you have scales to weigh the flesh?

zh: 一磅安东尼奥的肉是你的了。
en: A pound of Antonio's flesh is yours.

zh: 法庭已经决定了，这就是法律。
en: The court has decided it, and it is the law.

zh: 等一下，还有别的事情。
en: One minute, there is something else.

zh: 这份协议允许你取肉，但不允许取一滴血。
en: This agreement allows you flesh, but not a single drop of blood.

zh: 原话是“一磅肉”。
en: The exact words are "a pound of flesh".

zh: 所以，拿走你的报酬，拿走你的一磅肉。
en: So, take your payment, take your pound of flesh.

zh: 但当你割肉时，你不得取一滴血。
en: But when you cut it, you must not take one drop of blood.

zh: 如果你取了，根据法律，你的土地和钱财将成为威尼斯的财产。
en: If you do, your land and money will become the property of Venice, by law.

zh: 你可以自己读法律。
en: You can read the law yourself.

zh: 好吧。我接受第一个提议。付给我三倍于债务的金额。
en: All right. I accept the first offer. Pay me three times the amount of the debt.

zh: 等等！放债人必须得到完全的公正。他只被允许根据担保书的条款获得公正。
en: Wait! The money-lender must have total justice. He is only allowed justice according to the words of the guarantee.

zh: 我甚至不能拿回我的贷款吗？
en: Can I not even have the loan returned to me?

zh: 你只能根据担保书得到欠你的东西。拿着它，并承担责任。
en: You will only have what is owed you according to the guarantee. Take it, and accept the responsibility.

zh: 那就让魔鬼带他走吧！我无话可说了。
en: Then let the Devil take him! I have nothing more to say.

zh: 等等！你还有另一条法律要遵守。
en: Wait! You have another law to obey.

zh: 根据法律，如果一个外国人试图夺取威尼斯公民的生命，该外国人的一半财产将属于该公民。
en: According to the law, if a foreigner tries to take the life of a citizen of Venice, half of the foreigner's property belongs to that citizen.

zh: 另一半属于国家，而冒犯者的生命只能由公爵来赦免。
en: The other half belongs to the state and the offender's life can only be saved by the Duke.

zh: 你曾计划夺取这位公民的生命。
en: You have planned to take the life of this citizen.

zh: 你违反了法律。
en: You have broken the law.

zh: 因此，跪下，请求公爵的宽恕。
en: Fall to your knees, therefore, and ask the Duke for forgiveness.

zh: 我想让你看看我们态度之间的区别。
en: I want you to see the difference between our attitudes.

zh: 因此，在你请求之前，我就饶恕你的性命。
en: Therefore, I am offering you your life before you ask for it.

zh: 你的一半财产归安东尼奥，另一半归国家。
en: Half of your property goes to Antonio, the other half comes to the state.

zh: 不，拿走我的命吧！不要给我留下那些！
en: No, take my life! Do not leave me with that!

zh: 大人，如您允许，让他保留他的一半财产。
en: With your permission, my lord, let him keep one half of his property.

zh: 在他死后，我将把另一半给那位最近偷走了他女儿的绅士。
en: After his death, I will give the other half to the gentleman who recently stole his daughter.

zh: 他必须签署一份协议，在他死后将所有财产留给他的女儿和洛伦佐。
en: He must sign an agreement to leave everything to his daughter and Lorenzo after his death.

zh: 他会这样做，否则他将被处以死刑。
en: He will do this, or he will be punished with death.

zh: 你满意了吗，夏洛克？你怎么说？
en: Are you satisfied, Shylock? What do you say?

zh: 我满意了。请现在让我走吧。把文件送给我，我会签字的。
en: I am satisfied. Let me go now, please. Send the document to me and I will sign it.

zh: 去吧，但务必做到。
en: Go, but be sure to do it.

zh: 你们许多人可能都熟悉这个观点，即我们所有人都通过平均大约六个人与其他人相连。
en: Many of you may be familiar with the idea that all of us are connected to everyone else by an average of about six people.

zh: 它于20世纪20年代首次由匈牙利作家弗里杰什·卡林西提及，他讨论了社交网络。
en: It was first mentioned in the 1920s by a Hungarian author Frigyes Karinthy who discussed social networks.

zh: 1967年，一位名叫斯坦利·米尔格拉姆的美国社会学家尝试用一种新方法来检验这个理论，他称之为“小世界问题”。
en: In 1967, an American sociologist called Stanley Milgram tried using a new method to test the theory, which he called the "small-world problem".

zh: 该实验涉及一个居住在美国的随机人群样本，并给他们一个包裹以及一个在国内其他地方陌生人的姓名、工作和地点。
en: The experiment involved a random sample of people who lived in America and giving them a parcel as well as the name, job and location of a stranger somewhere else in the country.

zh: 实验的目的是让这个人尝试通过将包裹寄给他们认为可能认识这个陌生人的人，从而将包裹送达给这个陌生人。
en: The aim of the experiment was for the person to try to deliver the parcel to this stranger by sending it to someone they thought might know them.

zh: 那个人接着也做同样的事情，直到包裹到达它应该到达的人手中。
en: That person in turn did the same until the parcel reached the person it was supposed to.

zh: 尽管难以置信，但包裹送达这个人只用了大约五到七个人！
en: Although hard to believe, it only took about five to seven people for the package to reach the person!

zh: 结果被一家名为《今日心理学》的杂志发布和发表，并启发了“六度分隔”这个短语。
en: The results were released and published by a magazine called Psychology Today and inspired the phrase, "Six Degrees of Separation".

zh: 最近，我听了一个广播节目，它强调了一个关于社区精神力量的有趣故事。
en: Recently, I listened to a radio show which highlighted an interesting story about the power of community spirit.

zh: 它是关于美国一个名为罗塞托的小镇。
en: It was about a small town in America called Roseto.

zh: 在20世纪50年代，心脏病在美国是一种流行病，但研究人员发现罗塞托的心脏病发病率远低于国内其他地区。
en: In the 1950s, heart attacks were an epidemic in the USA, but researchers found Roseto had much lower rates of heart attacks than other places in the country.

zh: 这个小镇非常安全，犯罪率非常低。
en: The town was very safe and had a very low crime rate.

zh: 当他们深入研究其原因时，他们发现罗塞托是一个非常紧密的社区。
en: When they looked deeper into the reasons for this, they found that Roseto was a very close community.

zh: 家庭通常由三代人组成，住在一起，人们经常互相拜访，尊重老人，并组织许多社区活动。
en: Families typically consisted of three generations living together and the people visited each other frequently, respected elderly people and organised lots of community events.

zh: 不幸的是，随着时间的推移，社区发生了变化，因为年轻一代摒弃了传统价值观，搬到了城镇的郊区。
en: Unfortunately, over the years the community changed as the younger generation rejected traditional values and moved to outskirts of the town.

zh: 结果，健康问题和犯罪增加了。
en: As a result, health problems and crime increased.

zh: 通过这个故事，健康、幸福和社区之间的联系非常清晰。
en: The link between health, happiness and community is really clear through this story.

zh: 你能从罗塞托的故事中学到什么教训？
en: What lessons can you learn from Roseto's story?

zh: 你会为你的社区做些什么？
en: What would you do for your community?

zh: 在《绿山墙的安妮》的故事中，主角安妮是一个11岁的孤儿。
en: In the story Anne of Green Gables, the main character Anne was an 11-year-old orphan.

zh: 她被送到一所属于玛丽拉和马修的房子。
en: She was sent to a house belonging to Marilla and Matthew.

zh: 当她到达门口时，玛丽拉惊讶得愣住了，因为她原本期望被送来一个男孩。
en: When she arrived at the door, Marilla was frozen in amazement as she expected to be sent a boy.

zh: 玛丽拉告诉安妮，她可以和他们待在一起，直到事情被调查清楚。
en: Marilla told Anne that she could stay with them until the matter was investigated.

zh: 安妮听到她可能被送回去时非常情绪化，接着就扑到椅子上哭了起来。
en: Anne was very emotional when she heard she might be sent back and proceeded to throw herself on a chair and cry.

zh: 玛丽拉和马修不确定如何安慰她，但尽力而为。
en: Marilla and Matthew were unsure how to comfort her but try their best.

zh: 他们告诉她没必要哭，但安妮继续感到难过。
en: They told her there was no need to cry but Anne continued to be upset.

zh: 玛丽拉为安妮做了晚餐，尽管她因为没有胃口而拒绝吃。
en: Marilla made dinner for Anne, although she refused to eat as she had no appetite.

zh: 晚餐后，玛丽拉带安妮去一间卧室睡觉。
en: After dinner, Marilla took Anne to a bedroom to sleep.

zh: 安妮发现房间又冷又空。
en: Anne found the room cold and bare.

zh: 她甚至把白色的窗帘描述为“冰冷的”。
en: She even described the white curtains as "icy".

zh: 当安妮准备上床睡觉时，她又开始抽泣起来。
en: Anne began to sob again as she got ready for bed.

zh: 然后她钻进毯子下面，把它们拉过头顶。
en: She then dived under the blankets and pulled them over her head.

zh: 玛丽拉回来查看安妮的情况，并整理了她的衣服。
en: Marilla came back to check on Anne and tidied her clothes.

zh: 当她说“晚安”时，安妮从毯子下露出头来，并告诉她这是她度过的最糟糕的夜晚。
en: When she said "Good night", Anne revealed herself from under the blankets and told her it was the worst night she ever had.

zh: 前几天我遇到了一件奇怪的事情。
en: Something strange happened to me the other day.

zh: 当我查看我的电子邮件收件箱时，我看到一封邮件，是一个我不认识的女人发给我的。
en: When I looked at my email inbox, I saw an email that was sent to me from a woman whose name was not known to me.

zh: 我完全不知道她是谁！
en: I had no idea who she was!

zh: 这听起来并不奇怪！
en: That doesn't sound very strange!

zh: 去年我在校报工作时，也收到过很多不认识的人发来的邮件。
en: I was emailed by lots of people I didn't know last year when I was working on the school newspaper.

zh: 我知道，但奇怪的是，这封邮件本意是发给另一个和我同名的人看的！
en: I know, but the strange thing was that this email was intended to be read by someone else with the same name as me!

zh: 哦不！你怎么知道的？
en: Oh no! How could you tell?

zh: 因为邮件里邀请我去参加一个二十年前毕业的校友聚会！
en: Because in the email I was invited to go to a school reunion for people who had left school twenty years ago!

zh: 你回复那封邮件了吗？
en: Did you reply to the email?

zh: 我敢肯定，写邮件的人如果知道她的邮件当时正被一个女学生阅读，一定会很惊讶。
en: I'm sure the person who wrote the email would have been surprised that, at that moment, her email was being read by a schoolgirl.

zh: 是的，我给她回信解释说她的邮件被错误的人收到了。
en: Yes, I wrote back to her and explained that her email had been received by the wrong person.

zh: 她回信说她对这个错误感到非常抱歉，并说这封邮件现在会被重新发送给正确的人。
en: She wrote back to say that she was really sorry about the mistake and the email would now be resent to the right person.

zh: 以眼还眼只会让整个世界变瞎。
en: An eye for an eye will only make the whole world blind.

zh: 天下最柔软的东西能战胜天下最坚硬的东西。
en: The softest things in the world overcome the hardest things in the world.

zh: 和平无法通过武力维持；只能通过理解来实现。
en: Peace cannot be kept by force; it can only be achieved through understanding.

zh: 人们喜欢说冲突是在善与恶之间。
en: People like to say that the conflict is between good and evil.

zh: 真正的冲突是在真理与谎言之间。
en: The real conflict is between truth and lies.

zh: “风暴”摇滚乐队的鼓手马明不得不打包行李。
en: Ma Ming, drummer for the rock band "Storm", had to pack his bags.

zh: 在邻居们抱怨他扰乱安宁后，他搬离了租住的公寓。
en: He moved out of his rented flat after complaints from his neighbours about disturbing the peace.

zh: 因为是单身，马明经常在晚上举办派对，但最大的问题是他倾向于在深夜打鼓。
en: Being single, Ma Ming often held parties at night, but the biggest problem was his tendency to drum late at night.

zh: 马明的邻居们说，暴露在这样的噪音中让他们快疯了。
en: Ma Ming's neighbours said they were being driven mad being exposed to such noise.

zh: 房东说，如果他早知道马明是个鼓手，他就不会把公寓租给他了。
en: The flat-owner said if he had known that Ma Ming was a drummer, he wouldn't have rented the flat to him.

zh: 邻居们在他搬进来时很快就意识到他们有麻烦了。
en: The neighbours quickly realised they were in trouble when he moved in.

zh: 从那时起，他们很少能睡个整夜觉。
en: And from then on, they rarely got a full night's sleep.

zh: 他们不堵上耳朵就无法放松或看书。
en: They couldn't relax or read a book without plugging their ears.

zh: 一位邻居还声称马明对他十几岁的儿子有不良影响。
en: One neighbour also claimed that Ma Ming had a bad influence on his teenage son.

zh: 最后，社区委员会采取了行动。
en: In the end, the community council took action.

zh: “我们进行了投票，并达成了一项决议。
en: "We took a vote, and came to a resolution.

zh: 我们给了马明一个警告。
en: We gave Ma Ming a warning.

zh: 我们告诉他，他应该停止打鼓或者离开这个房产，”一位委员会成员说。
en: We told him that he ought to cease drumming or leave the property," a council member said.

zh: “获得足够的睡眠对人们的健康很重要，在这样一片抱怨声之后，我们不得不采取行动。”
en: "Getting enough sleep is important for people's health and, after such a chorus of complaints, we had to take action."

zh: 马明的离开让他的邻居们很高兴。
en: Ma Ming's departure has pleased his neighbours.

zh: “现在生活将恢复正常，”他们说。
en: "Life will go back to normal now," they said.

zh: 为了了解马明对此事的说法，我们在上海的一家酒店找到了他。
en: For Ma Ming's version of the story, we found him in a hotel in Shanghai.

zh: 马明觉得他们对他有偏见。
en: Ma Ming felt that they were prejudiced against him.

zh: 他对人们把他的音乐视为“噪音”这一事实感到愤懑，否则他并不真的介意必须离开公寓。
en: He's bitter over the fact that people thought of his music as "noise", otherwise he didn't really mind having to leave the flat.

zh: “住在酒店意味着酒店员工每天铺床，而且我不必自己洗衣服！
en: "Living in a hotel means the hotel staff makes the bed every day and I don't have to do my own washing!

zh: 不管怎样，我现在正在寻找一座位于城市边缘的偏僻房子。”
en: Anyhow, I'm now looking for a remote house on the edge of the city."

zh: 80岁的退休裁缝詹姆斯·麦凯因用拐杖击打30岁的基思·史密斯的头部，在牢房里度过了周六夜晚。
en: Eighty-year-old retired tailor, James McKay, spent Saturday night in a cell after hitting 30-year-old Keith Smith over the head with his walking stick.

zh: 麦凯的妻子劳伦说，尽管麦凯通常是个平和愉快的人，但他确实因为被淋湿太多次而被逼采取了这种暴力行为。
en: McKay's wife, Laurene said that, while McKay is usually a peaceful and pleasant person, he had been driven to this act of violence by literally getting wet just once too often.

zh: 他再也无法忍受了。
en: He could no longer tolerate it.

zh: 史密斯住在麦凯家楼上。
en: Smith lives above the McKays.

zh: 他是一位热心的园丁，也是一位鱼类收藏家。
en: He is a keen gardener, and also a fish collector.

zh: 不幸的是，他每天从阳台泼出的水最终都流到了麦凯家的地板上，或者太多次，直接淋到了毫无防备的麦凯夫妇身上。
en: Unfortunately, the water he sent over his balcony every day ended up on the McKays' floor, or too often, on the unsuspecting McKays themselves.

zh: “在过去两周，自从史密斯搬到我们楼上的公寓，我们就不敢去我们的阳台了，”劳伦说。
en: "For the last two weeks, since Smith moved into the flat above us, we dared not go onto our balcony," said Laurene.

zh: 她补充说，困扰他们的不仅是史密斯浇花时落到他们阳台上的水，还有他清洗鱼缸的方式。
en: She added that it wasn't only the water falling onto their balcony from Smith watering his plants that bothered them, but also the way he cleaned his fish tanks.

zh: “我们会坐在那里愉快地看报纸，突然之间会有大量的水从上面浇下来，把我们淋得就像穿着衣服洗了澡一样！
en: "We'd be sitting there happily reading our newspapers, when suddenly so much water would come from above that we'd be as wet as if we had showered with our clothes on!

zh: 我们几乎无法摆脱鱼腥味！”
en: We could hardly get rid of the smell of fish!"

zh: 而在周六晚上，这实在是太过分了。
en: And on Saturday evening it was just too much.

zh: “那天是詹姆斯的生日，”劳伦回忆道，“那是一个欣赏外面星空的美丽夜晚。
en: "It was James' birthday," Laurene recalled, "and it was such a beautiful night to enjoy the starry night outside.

zh: 我给他做了一个生日蛋糕。
en: I made him a birthday cake.

zh: 蜡烛的景象非常壮观，你可以想象，但詹姆斯没能吹灭它们。”
en: The candles were a great sight as you can imagine, but James didn't get to blow them out."

zh: 相反，史密斯将他一个较大的鱼缸里的水从阳台上倒了下来，麦凯夫妇和蛋糕都湿透了。
en: Instead, Smith emptied one of his larger tanks over his balcony and both the McKays and the cake were wet through.

zh: “我从未见过他动作如此之快，我无法阻止他。
en: "I have never seen him move so fast and I couldn't stop him.

zh: 他一下子就冲上去了。”
en: He was up there in a flash."

zh: 史密斯不打算就此事再与警方进一步纠缠。
en: Smith is not going to take things further with the police.

zh: 他还承诺从现在开始改变他的行为方式。
en: He has also promised to change his ways from now on.

zh: 那么詹姆斯·麦凯呢？
en: And what of James McKay?

zh: 当他离开警察局时，一大群支持者为他唱起了“生日快乐”。
en: As he left the police station, a large crowd of supporters sang him "Happy Birthday".

zh: “绝对是有史以来最激动人心的生日！”这位开朗的老人说。
en: "Definitely the most exciting birthday ever!" said the cheerful old man.

zh: “要我说，这是我年轻时以来最好的一次！”
en: "The best since my youth, I'd say!"

zh: 艾米数学考试没考好，结果她妈妈对她非常生气。
en: Amy did not do well in her maths exam, and her mother was very angry with her as a result.

zh: 艾米争辩说一次考试并不意味着彻底失败，但她妈妈不同意。
en: Amy argued that one exam would not mean total failure, but her mother did not agree.

zh: 艾米处理这个问题的方式是拒绝和她妈妈说话三天。
en: Amy's way to deal with the problem was refusing to talk to her mother for three days.

zh: 鲍勃在公共汽车上不小心踩到了一个男人的脚。
en: Bob accidentally stamped on a man's foot on the bus.

zh: 那个男人非常生气，对着鲍勃咒骂。
en: The man was very angry and swore at Bob.

zh: 他对着那个男人大喊大叫并骂了回去。
en: He yelled and swore back at the man.

zh: 黛西认为是凯西向老师告发了她，尽管凯西并没有这么做。
en: Daisy thought Cathy had reported her to the teacher, even though Cathy had not.

zh: 黛西非常生气，以至于她不允许凯西讲述她那一方的说法。
en: Daisy was so angry that she wouldn't allow Cathy to tell her side of the story.

zh: 因此，凯西请她的朋友菲奥娜代表她去和黛西谈。
en: Therefore, Cathy asked her friend Fiona to talk to Daisy on her behalf.

zh: 杜竹是一名48岁的越南农民，他的两个女儿和一位姑妈在那天被美国士兵在美莱村杀害。
en: Do Chuc is a 48-year-old Vietnamese farmer whose two daughters and an aunt were killed by American soldiers in My Lai that day.

zh: 他和他的家人正在吃早餐，这时美国士兵进入村庄，命令所有平民离开他们的家。
en: He and his family were eating breakfast when the American soldiers entered the village and ordered all civilians out of their homes.

zh: 他们和其他村民一起被押着走了几百米到村子的广场，在那里他们被告知坐下。
en: Together with other villagers, they were marched a few hundred metres into the village square where they were told to sit.

zh: “我们仍然没有理由害怕，”竹回忆道。
en: "Still we had no reason to be afraid," Chuc remembered.

zh: 我们以前都见过这种场面。”
en: We'd seen it all before."

zh: 然后他惊讶地看着士兵们架起一挺机枪。
en: Then he watched in surprise as the soldiers set up a machine gun.

zh: 平静结束了，恐慌开始了。
en: The calm ended and panic set in.

zh: 人们开始哭泣和祈祷。
en: The people began weeping and praying.

zh: 一个人向一名士兵出示了他的身份证件，但那个美国人只是说，“对不起。”
en: One man showed his identification papers to a soldier, but the American simply said, "Sorry."

zh: 竹的腿部受伤，几乎失去知觉，但他被一堆尸体覆盖，因此，他的生命得救了。
en: Chuc was wounded in the leg and almost unconscious, but he was covered by a pile of dead bodies and thus, his life was saved.

zh: 等了一个小时后，他逃离了村庄。
en: After waiting an hour, he fled the village.

zh: 我们在前线，在圣诞节的早晨，我们竖起了一块牌子，上面写着“圣诞快乐”。
en: We were on the frontier and on Christmas morning we stuck up a board displaying "A Merry Christmas" on it.

zh: 敌人也竖起了一块类似的牌子。
en: The enemy had stuck up a similar one.

zh: 然后我们的两个人扔掉了他们的装备，双手举过头顶爬出战壕，作为我们的代表。
en: Two of our men then threw their equipment off and climbed out of the trench with their hands above their heads as our representatives.

zh: 他们互相问候并握手。
en: They greeted each other and shook hands.

zh: 然后我们都爬出了战壕。
en: Then we all got out of the trench.

zh: 比尔（我们的军官）试图阻止，但为时已晚，所以他和其他的军官们也爬了出来。
en: Bill (our officer) tried to prevent it but it was too late, so he and the other officers climbed out, too.

zh: 我们和德国人穿过泥地，在无人区的中间相遇了。
en: We, and the Germans, walked through the mud and met in the middle of no-man's land.

zh: 我们一整天都待在一起。
en: We spent all day with one another.

zh: 他们中的一些人会说英语。
en: Some of them could speak English.

zh: 从他们的样子来看，他们的战壕状况和我们的一样糟糕。
en: By the look of them, their trenches were in as bad a state as our own.

zh: 他们中的一个人用英语说，他曾在英国工作过几年，他对这场战争厌烦到了极点，战争结束他会很高兴。
en: One of their men, speaking in English, said that he had worked in England for some years and that he was fed up to the neck with this war and would be glad when it was over.

zh: 我们告诉他，他不是唯一一个对此感到厌烦的人。
en: We told him he wasn't the only one who was fed up with it.

zh: 德国军官问比尔我们是否想要一些啤酒，然后他们把啤酒拿给了我们。
en: The German officer asked Bill if we would like some beer and they brought them over to us.

zh: 比尔把啤酒分给我们，我们喝了很多。
en: Bill distributed the beer among us and we consumed a lot.

zh: 军官们达成共识，我们将在暂时的和平中庆祝圣诞节，直到午夜。
en: The officers came to an understanding that we would celebrate Christmas in temporary peace until midnight.

zh: 就在午夜之前，我们都决定不在他们开火之前先开火。
en: Just before midnight, we all decided not to start firing before they did.

zh: 我们形成了一种纽带，在整个节礼日期间，我们一枪未发，他们也一样；双方似乎都在等待对方先开始。
en: We'd formed a bond and during the whole of Boxing Day, we never fired a shot and they the same; each side seemed to be waiting for the other to set the ball rolling.

zh: 他们中的一个人用英语喊话，问我们觉得啤酒怎么样。
en: One of their men shouted across in English and asked how we had enjoyed the beer.

zh: 我们回答说我们非常感激，并和他们聊了一整天。
en: We replied that we were very grateful and spent the whole day chatting with them.

zh: 那天晚上，我们被其他士兵替换了。
en: That evening we were replaced by other soldiers.

zh: 我接到护士长的电话，说：“你那里有一个病人将要获得一个奖项。
en: I got a phone Call from the chief nurse, saying, "You've got a patient there who is going to get an award.

zh: 确保病房看起来不错。”
en: Make sure that the ward looks good."

zh: 这从一开始就让我很反感，“让我们打扫病房吧，因为有重要人物要来了。”
en: This really turned me off to begin with, "Let's clean up the ward because we've got VIPs coming in."

zh: 嗯，这些重要人物碰巧是一位将军和大约十几个人。
en: Well, the VIPs happened to be a general and a group of about a dozen people.

zh: 这是这位病人第二次来我们这里，这次他的双腿都被炸掉了——他大约20岁。
en: It was this patient's second visit to us and this time he'd had both his legs blown off — he was all-of-about 20 years old.

zh: 当他在外科医生手术后醒来时，他低声说：“您不记得我了吗，女士？”
en: When he was waking up after the surgeon had finished, he whispered, "Don't you remember me, ma'am?"

zh: 但事实上我不记得，因为病人太多了。
en: But really I didn't because there were so many of them.

zh: 将军来给他颁奖，因为他恰好是第20,000个来到这家医院的人。
en: The general was coming to give him the award because he happened to be number 20,000 to come through this hospital.

zh: 他们举行了一个小仪式，在仪式上授予他一枚紫心勋章和一块手表。
en: They had this little ceremony, where they presented him with a Purple Heart and a watch.

zh: 当将军把表递给他，并说“来自军队，以表示我们的感谢”时，那个孩子或多或少地把表扔回给了他。
en: As the general handed him the watch, "from the army, to show our appreciation," the kid more or less threw the watch back at him.

zh: 他说了类似这样的话：“我不能接受这个，先生；它不能帮助我走路。”
en: He said something like, "I can't accept this, sir; it's not going to help me walk."

zh: 在这个小事件之后，我走过去把他抱在怀里。
en: After this little incident, I went over and took him in my arms.

zh: 如果我没记错的话，我开始抽泣，我想他也在哭。
en: If I remember correctly, I started sobbing and I think he was crying, too.

zh: 我真的很佩服他那样做。
en: I really admired him for that.

zh: 那是唯一一次我让别人看到我的感受。
en: That was the only time I let somebody see what I felt.

zh: 他这样做需要很大的勇气，这在一定程度上向我说明了这场战争的全部意义。
en: It took a lot for him to do that, and it sort of said what this war was all about to me.

zh: 战争期间，我不得不在南非的一个空军基地工作。
en: During the war, I had to work on an air base in South Africa.

zh: 我记得有一次我们有24小时的休假，我和朋友们在镇上玩了一晚。
en: I remember once we had 24 hours off-duty, my friends and I had a night out in the town.

zh: 大约午夜时分，我们正慢慢走回基地，谈论着晚上的事，这时有人提到了南非著名的野生动物。
en: We were walking slowly back to the base at about midnight, talking about the evening, when someone mentioned South Africa's famous wild animals.

zh: 我们正在闲聊听到的故事，突然我的朋友鲍勃恳求我们安静。
en: We were chatting about stories we'd heard when suddenly my friend, Bob, begged us to be quiet.

zh: “你自己安静点！”有人回答。
en: "Be quiet yourself!" somebody replied.

zh: “不，大家都安静，”我低声说。
en: "No, everyone be quiet," I whispered.

zh: 然后我们都听到了狮子的声音。
en: And then we all could hear the lions.

zh: 我们都朝我们的基地跑去。
en: We all ran towards our base.

zh: 除了我，每个人都成功返回了——我掉进了一个壕沟！
en: Everyone made it back except me — I fell into a trench!

zh: 我在那里一直待到早上，然后爬出来走回基地。
en: I stayed there till morning when I climbed out and walked back to the base.

zh: 我的朋友们像欢迎英雄一样迎接我。
en: My friends greeted me like a hero.

zh: 他们以为我被吃了！
en: They thought I'd been eaten!

zh: 我发誓我再也不会在晚上喝完酒然后在野外行走了。
en: I swore that I'd never go drinking and then walking in the wild at night again.

zh: 在许多国家，已经有法律不允许为香烟等烟草产品做广告。
en: In many countries, there are already laws which do not allow advertising for tobacco products like cigarettes.

zh: 在我看来，我们应该更进一步，禁止为任何不健康的产品做广告，例如酒精和油炸食品。
en: In my opinion, we should go further and ban advertisements for any unhealthy products such as alcohol and fried food.

zh: 毫无疑问，不健康的产品危害我们的健康。
en: Undoubtedly, unhealthy products harm our health.

zh: 限制快餐的电视广告显然是可取的。
en: It is clearly desirable to limit TV advertisements for fast food.

zh: 肥胖率已大幅上升。
en: The rate of obesity has increased greatly.

zh: 在一些国家，它是仅次于吸烟的死亡原因。
en: In some countries, it is second only to smoking as a cause of death.

zh: 此外，饮酒会导致多种疾病。
en: In addition, drinking alcohol results in a wide range of diseases.

zh: 如果孩子们在电视上看到更少的人做这些活动的广告，他们尝试模仿这些行为的可能性就会小得多。
en: If children see fewer advertisements on TV of people doing these activities, they are much less likely to try to copy their behaviour.

zh: 人们反对这个想法的一个原因是，生产这些产品创造了就业机会并带来了大量的税收。
en: A reason why people are against the idea is that making the products creates jobs and brings in large amounts of tax.

zh: 然而，这肯定是一个错误的论点。
en: However, surely this is a false argument.

zh: 投资于生产这些不健康产品的资金可以用于更有价值的事业，例如那些改善我们环境的事业。
en: The money invested in making these unhealthy products could be used for more worthwhile businesses, such as those that improve our environment.

zh: 此外，从危害人类健康的活动中赚钱是不对的。
en: Furthermore, it is not right to make money from activities which harm human health.

zh: 总之，禁止不健康产品电视广告的法律对社会是有益的。
en: To conclude, laws to ban TV advertisements of unhealthy products are beneficial to society.

zh: 它们有助于拯救生命并改善国民的健康。
en: They help to save lives and improve the health of a nation.

zh: 美国妇女特丽·夏沃的近期案例暴露了围绕昏迷病人护理的许多敏感法律和医疗问题，她的丈夫在她处于昏迷状态15年后赢得了一场法律诉讼，让她撤下了生命维持机器。
en: The recent case of an American woman, Terri Schiavo, whose husband won a legal battle to have her taken off life support machines after she spent 15 years in a coma-like state, has exposed the many sensitive legal and medical issues that surround the care of coma patients.

zh: 尽管15年过去了，特丽的父母仍然相信她的昏迷只是暂时的。
en: Even though 15 years had passed, Terri's parents still believed that her coma was only temporary.

zh: 她的丈夫不同意，并表示特丽不会想以这种状态活下去。
en: Her husband disagreed and said that Terri would not have wanted to live on in this state.

zh: 法院批准关闭生命维持机器。
en: The court gave their approval for the life support machines to be turned off.

zh: 这一裁决让特丽的父母感到麻木。
en: This ruling left Terri's parents feeling numb.

zh: 其他人声称生命是神圣的，没有人有权结束它，但许多人会说这对特丽来说是正确的事情。
en: Others claimed that life is sacred, which no one has the right to end, but many would say it was the right thing to do for Terri.

zh: 特丽陷入昏迷是因为一次心脏病发作剥夺了她大脑的氧气，造成了永久性伤害。
en: Terri had fallen into a coma when a heart attack robbed her brain of oxygen causing permanent harm.

zh: 人们也可能因癌症等绝症和头部受伤而陷入昏迷。
en: People can also fall into comas due to terminal illnesses like cancer and head injuries.

zh: 昏迷病人可能看起来一直在睡觉，或者他们可能睡觉、醒来、移动和发出声音。
en: Coma patients may seem to be asleep all the time or they may sleep, wake up, move and make sounds.

zh: 尽管病人有时可能看起来“醒着”，但他们的大脑仍然无意识，无法被唤醒。
en: Even though the patient may seem to be "awake" at times, their brain remains unconscious and cannot be woken up.

zh: 尽管医生不完全了解昏迷和恢复过程，但人们普遍认为，病人在昏迷中停留的时间越长，恢复的机会就越小。
en: Although doctors don't fully understand comas and the process of recovery, it is generally agreed that a patient's chances of recovery decline the longer they stay in a coma.

zh: 因此，15年后，特丽恢复的可能性非常小。
en: Therefore, after 15 years it was very unlikely that Terri would ever recover.

zh: 即使在不太严重的情况下，医生也承认他们不知道病人是否会好转或好转的程度如何。
en: Even in less severe cases, doctors admit that they don't know if or how well patients will progress.

zh: 当对大脑的伤害最小且昏迷时间短暂时，病人通常能恢复正常，但会有一些记忆或其他脑功能的丧失。
en: When there is a minimum of harm to the brain and the coma is brief, patients often return to normal but with some loss of memory or other brain functions.

zh: 当对大脑的伤害更严重且昏迷时间更长时，一些病人恢复缓慢，而其他人在数年内几乎没有或没有任何进展。
en: When the harm to the brain is worse and the coma is longer, some patients recover slowly while others show little or no progress over a period of years.

zh: 什么是正确的决定？
en: What is the right decision?

zh: 对于长期昏迷状态的情况，是否应该允许近亲将他们所爱的人撤下生命维持机器？
en: In the case of long-term coma states, should close family members be allowed to take their loved one off life support machines?

zh: 想象一下你今天正走出教室。
en: Imagine you are on your way out of class today.

zh: 你被一块松动的地毯绊倒，扭伤了脚踝。
en: You trip on a loose piece of carpet and twist your ankle.

zh: 由于这次受伤，你失去了在当地运动队的位置，并且不得不错过一个重要的工作面试。
en: As a result of the injury, you lose your place in the local sports team, and have to miss an important job interview.

zh: 是运气不好？还是难免的事？抑或是一个快速致富的机会？
en: Bad luck? Just one of those things? Or an opportunity to get rich quickly?

zh: 休斯顿的罗斯林·达奇被一个在家具店乱跑的小孩绊倒并摔断了脚踝，她感到恼火，这或许并不令人惊讶。
en: Perhaps it's not surprising that Roslyn Darch of Houston felt annoyed when she tripped over a little kid running around a furniture store, and broke her ankle.

zh: 但几个月后，在成功起诉了那家商店后，她变得更有钱了，获得了78万美元。
en: But a few months later, she was $780,000 richer, after successfully suing the shop.

zh: 店主对罗斯林获得的赔偿金额显然感到惊讶，尤其是因为她绊倒的那个孩子是她自己的儿子。
en: The owners were clearly surprised at the size of Roslyn's payout, particularly since the child she tripped over was her own son.

zh: 一些人认为这只是贪婪——这笔钱相对于所受的伤害来说实在太多了——而且，母亲管不住自己的孩子本来就不是商店的错。
en: Some argue that this is just greed — that the amount of money is far too much for the injury suffered — and besides, it's not the shop's fault that a mother can't control her child anyway.

zh: 另一些人则会说，看到法律站在个人一边对抗大公司，这倒是个变化，是件好事。
en: Others would say that it's good to see the law taking the side of the individual against the big corporations, for a change.

zh: 以数百名吸烟者为例，他们声称烟草公司应对他们因吸烟而患上的绝症负责，并因此从烟草公司获得了数百万美元。
en: Take the example of the hundreds of smokers who have received millions of dollars from the tobacco companies, after saying they were responsible for the terminal illnesses they had developed because of smoking.

zh: 不管你怎么想，近年来出现的索赔文化正在蔓延，它将影响到我们所有人。
en: Whatever you think, the compensation culture which has emerged in recent years is spreading, and it will affect all of us.

zh: 而且，让律师们忙碌的不仅仅是人身伤害索赔。
en: And it's not only claims for physical injuries that are keeping the lawyers busy.

zh: 一群超重的纽约青少年起诉了一家大型快餐公司，声称他们没有得到足够的警告，说明饮食中的汉堡、薯条和奶昔会让他们发胖。
en: A group of overweight New York teenagers sued a giant fast-food company, claiming that they had not had enough warning that a diet of burgers, fries and milkshakes would make them fat.

zh: 一位19岁英国女学生的父母在她未能获得大学入学考试的最高分时，成功起诉了她的学校，获得了42,000英镑的赔偿；并且，也许在所有案例中最奇怪的是，桑德拉·约克因踩到洒落的软饮料而滑倒后，从一家餐厅获得了113,000美元。
en: The parents of a 19-year-old English school girl successfully sued her school for £42,000 compensation when she failed to get a top grade in a university entrance exam; and, in perhaps the most weird case of all, Sandra York received $113,000 from a restaurant after slipping on a spilt soft drink.

zh: 然而，饮料在地板上是因为约克在30秒前与男友争吵时把它扔向了男友。
en: However, the drink was on the floor because York had thrown it over her boyfriend 30 seconds earlier, during an argument.

zh: 如果你认为去度假是摆脱所有这些麻烦的好方法，请再想一想——每年有数百万人向英国旅游管理局投诉，许多人获得了某种形式的赔偿。
en: If you think that going on holiday is a good way to get away from all this trouble, think again — millions of people complain to the British Tourist Authority every year, and many receive some form of compensation.

zh: 一家著名公司的工作人员习惯于处理因恶劣天气、航班取消和行李丢失而提出的赔偿请求，但一位发言人说：“我们最近收到一个人的索赔，他说他们的假期被毁了，因为他们与旅伴相处不好。”
en: Staff at a famous company is used to dealing with requests for compensation following poor weather, cancelled flights and lost luggage, but one spokesman said, "we recently had a claim from someone who said their holiday was ruined because they didn't get on with their travelling companion.

zh: 我们经常收到出国旅游的度假者的投诉，他们说当地人不会说英语。”
en: And we regularly get complaints from holidaymakers travelling abroad who say the locals don't speak English."

zh: 谁知道这将在哪里结束？
en: Who knows where it will end?

zh: 有人说应该对过度的索赔进行处罚，或者应该对赔付设置上限。
en: Some say there should be penalties for excessive claims, or that there should be a limit on payouts.

zh: 但有一件事是肯定的——最终，唯一确定的赢家是律师！
en: But one thing's for sure — in the end, the only certain winner is the lawyer!

zh: 现代历史上有如此多的冲突导致了生命的丧失。
en: There have been so many conflicts in modern history that have led to loss of life.

zh: 我最近在网上看了一部展示战争恐怖的纪录片。
en: I recently watched a documentary online that showed the horror of war.

zh: 它是关于第一次世界大战的，数百万年轻人在战壕中相互战斗了多年。
en: It was about World War I, where millions of young men spent years in trenches fighting each other.

zh: 有一位英国将军带领着一群士兵。
en: There was a British general who was leading a group of soldiers.

zh: 他们驻扎在法国东部战争的前线附近。
en: They were based near the frontier of the war in Eastern France.

zh: 他们试图保护一个村庄里的平民。
en: They were trying to protect civilians in a village.

zh: 一天晚上，许多德国炸弹被投掷在该地区周围。
en: One night, many German bombs were dropped around the area.

zh: 村民们惊慌失措，跑进了黑夜中。
en: The villagers panicked and ran into the night.

zh: 他们全部被机枪火力杀死。
en: They were all killed by machine gun fire.

zh: 这一事件给将军带来了巨大的痛苦。
en: The incident caused the general so much suffering.

zh: 他作为一位老人接受了采访，并在讲述时哭泣。
en: He was interviewed as an elderly man and was weeping as he spoke about it.

zh: 尽管这是一部难以观看的纪录片，但我觉得它让我对和平的价值有了更深的认识。
en: Although it was a tough documentary to watch, I feel it gave me an appreciation for the value of peace.

zh: 没有任何战争或冲突，世界将会变得更美好。
en: The world would be a better place without any war or conflict.

zh: 第二次世界大战结束时，我们在罗马。
en: We were in Rome when World War II ended.

zh: 我们第一次知道这件事是当我们听到钟声响起，人们跑到街上时。
en: The first we knew of it was when we heard bells ringing and people running into the streets.

zh: 每个人都因喜悦和兴奋而大喊。
en: Everyone was shouting with joy and excitement.

zh: “发生什么事了？”我问道。
en: "What's going on?" I asked.

zh: “似乎战争结束了，”一位士兵回答道。
en: "It seems like the war is over," replied one of the soldiers.

zh: 每个人都立刻开始说话。
en: Everyone started speaking at once.

zh: 一些人开始因喜悦和宽慰而哭泣。
en: Some people began weeping with joy and relief.

zh: 其他人互相告诉对方，这是一个将载入史册的日子。
en: Others were telling each other that it was a day that they would go down in history.

zh: 那天晚些时候，当盟军坦克驶入广场时，我们向它们致意。
en: Later that day we greeted the allied tanks as they rolled into the square.

zh: 这是一个充满巨大喜悦的日子，一个我永远不会忘记的日子！
en: It was a day of great joy and one I will never forget!

zh: 不久前，我哥哥和我经常吵架。
en: Awhile ago my brother and I were quarrelling a lot.

zh: 起初我们每周至少争吵一次，但后来这简直成了家常便饭。
en: At first we would argue at least once a week but then it literally became a daily occurrence.

zh: 他总是取笑我，尽管我尽量不对他做出反应，但通常很难做到。
en: He was always teasing me and although I tried not to react to him, it was usually difficult not to.

zh: 我记不起我们所有争吵的原因，但我记得有一次他没问我就拿走了我的手机，用它给他的朋友打电话。
en: I can't recall the reason for all our arguments, but I remember once he took my mobile phone without asking and used it to make calls to his friends.

zh: 我不敢相信他竟敢做那样的事！
en: I couldn't believe he would dare do something like that!

zh: 我没有和他打架，而是决定完全疏远他，假装他不存在。
en: Instead of fighting with him, I decided then to withdraw from him completely and just pretend he didn't exist.

zh: 我以为这会有所帮助，但情况变得更糟了。
en: I thought this would help but the situation became worse.

zh: 最后，我决定通过告诉他我的感受来处理冲突。
en: In the end, I decided to handle the conflict by telling him how I was feeling.

zh: 他很惊讶，因为他没有意识到他如此严重地伤害了我。
en: He was surprised as he didn't realise he had hurt me so much.

zh: 不管怎样，我们和好了，从那时起，他一直对我很友好和愉快。
en: Anyhow we made peace with each other and since then he has always been friendly and pleasant to me.

zh: 当你和别人有问题时，沟通总是最好的方式。
en: Communication is always the best thing when you have a problem with someone.

zh: 我在学校过得很艰难。
en: I'm having a hard time at school.

zh: 有一群人一直在欺负我。
en: There is a group that keeps bullying me.

zh: 你认为我应该怎么做？
en: What do you think I should do?

zh: 你应该记住的第一件事是，欺负别人的人自尊心很低，他们这样做只是因为他们对自己感觉不好。
en: The first thing you should remember is that people who bully have low self-esteem and they only do it because they don't feel good about themselves.

zh: 如果你意识到这不是你的问题，你就已经解决了一半的问题。
en: If you realise that it's not about you, you are halfway to solving the problem.

zh: 嗯，你这么说有道理，但仅仅忽视他们并不容易。
en: Hmm, that makes sense when you say it, but it isn't easy to just ignore them.

zh: 我不是叫你忽视他们。
en: I'm not telling you to ignore them.

zh: 事实上，我认为你应该勇敢地面对他们。
en: In fact, I think you should stand up to them.

zh: 是啊，他们比我高大那么多，我该怎么做到呢？
en: Yeah, how am I going to do that when they are so much bigger than I am?

zh: 体型与此无关。
en: Size has nothing to do with it.

zh: 重要的是你对自己的感觉。
en: It's how you feel about yourself that matters.

zh: 意识到你是一个多么了不起的人，然后下次，当他们试图欺负你时，告诉他们你不再忍受了。
en: Realise what an amazing person you are and then next time, when they try to bully you, tell them you are not going to put up with it anymore.

zh: 如果那没有用......
en: And if that doesn't work ...

zh: 那么可能是时候告诉大人了。
en: Then it is probably time to tell an adult about it.

zh: 偶尔，一项新技术、一个老问题和一个伟大的想法会变成一项创新。
en: Every once in a while, a new technology, an old problem, and a big idea turn into an innovation.

zh: 智慧是适应变化的能力。
en: Intelligence is the ability to adapt to change.

zh: 我是那些像诺贝尔一样思考的人之一，认为人类将从新发现中汲取的善多于恶。
en: I am one of those who think like Nobel, that humanity will draw more good than evil from new discoveries.

zh: 创新区分了领导者和追随者。
en: Innovation distinguishes between a leader and a follower.

zh: 如果你必须选择20世纪唯一最重要的发现，你会面临一个真正的问题。
en: If you had to choose the single most important discovery of the 20th century, you would have a real problem on your hands.

zh: 在短短100年内，世界已经完全改变了。
en: In just 100 years, the world has changed completely.

zh: 在医学、通信和交通领域取得了惊人的发现，更不用说我们对世界和太空的认识了。
en: Amazing discoveries were made in medicine, communications and transport, not to mention our knowledge of the world and space.

zh: 医学的进步范围从在显微镜下发现疾病的原因到进行手术用捐赠的器官替换病变的器官。
en: Medical advances ranged from discovering the causes of diseases under microscopes to staging operations to replace diseased organs with donated ones.

zh: 通信随着手机的引入而改变，我们通信的方式从写信变成了发电子邮件和发送即时消息。
en: Communications changed with the introduction of mobile phones, and the way we correspond went from writing letters to emailing and sending instant messages.

zh: 我们开始在世界各地飞行，将卫星发射到轨道上，与此同时，科学家们想出了如何分裂原子，这以前被认为是宇宙中最小的物质粒子。
en: We started flying around the world, launching satellites into orbit and, at the same time, scientists figured out how to split the atom, previously thought to be the smallest particle of matter in the universe.

zh: 虽然不可能选择最重要的发现，但有可能挑选出20世纪的一些先驱者。
en: Although it is impossible to choose the most important discovery, it is possible to single out a few pioneers of the 20th century.

zh: 以下是其中一些。
en: Here are some of them.

zh: 20世纪最重要的科学家之一是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦。
en: One of the 20th century's premier scientists was Albert Einstein.

zh: 1905年夏天，这位直言不讳的年轻人正在摇他一岁的孩子，突然受到了启发。
en: In the summer of 1905, this outspoken young man was rocking his one-year-old baby when he was suddenly inspired.

zh: 它显示了一小块质量如何能产生难以置信的能量。
en: It showed how a small piece of mass could produce an unbelievable amount of energy.

zh: 然后爱因斯坦在他的“相对论”中表明，甚至时间、质量或长度都不是恒定的——它们根据我们对它们的体验而变化。
en: Einstein then showed in his "theory of relativity" that not even time, mass or length are constant — they change according to our experience of them.

zh: 1929年，生物学家亚历山大·弗莱明做出了另一项重要发现。
en: In 1929, another important finding was made by biologist Alexander Fleming.

zh: 在他去度假之前，弗莱明在实验室里留下了一盘细菌。
en: Before he went on holiday, Fleming left a dish of bacteria in his laboratory.

zh: 当他回来时，他注意到一些奇怪的事情。
en: When he came back, he noticed something strange.

zh: 他仔细检查，看到盘子里有一种蓝色的霉菌，周围的细菌已经被破坏了。
en: He double-checked and saw a blue mould in the dish around which the bacteria had been destroyed.

zh: 细菌已被消灭。
en: the bacteria had been destroyed.

zh: 这种蓝色霉菌实际上是青霉素的自然形态，弗莱明意识到它可以用来杀死细菌。
en: This blue mould was in fact the natural form of penicillin, which Fleming perceived could be used to kill bacteria.

zh: 几年后，青霉素开始大规模生产，并帮助拯救了数百万人的生命。
en: A few years later, penicillin was being mass-produced and helping to save the lives of millions.

zh: 弗莱明对他这一发现的惊人成果保持谦逊。
en: Fleming remained humble about the amazing outcome of his discovery.

zh: “是大自然制造了青霉素，”他说，“我只是发现了它。”
en: "Nature made penicillin," he said, "I just found it."

zh: 第二次世界大战期间，当弗莱明的发现首次帮助治愈病人时，美国海军正在寻找提高导弹精度的方法。
en: During World War II, when Fleming's discovery was first helping to cure people, the US Navy was looking for ways of improving the accuracy of their missiles.

zh: 海军求助于工程师埃克特和物理学家莫奇利，让他们共同处理这个问题并制造一台机器来完成这项工作。
en: The navy turned to Eckert, an engineer, and Mauchly, a physicist, to deal with the problem and produce a machine to do the job in a joint effort.

zh: 尽管他们直到1946年战争结束后才完成，但这并不重要。
en: Although they only finished after the war in 1946, it did not matter.

zh: 这台巨大的机器是世界上第一台计算机，但它与我们今天的计算机完全不同。
en: This huge machine was the world's first computer, but it was nothing like our computers today.

zh: 它长100英尺，高超过10英尺，重量超过30,000公斤。
en: It measured 100 feet long by over 10 feet high and weighed over 30,000 kilograms.

zh: 它拥有18,000个电子管、数千个电路和6,000个开关，耗能巨大，以至于当它启动时，当地城镇的灯都熄灭了！
en: With 18,000 tubes, thousands of circuits and 6,000 switches, it used so much energy that when it was turned on, the lights in the local town went out!

zh: 随着计算机的发展，人们期望能更高效地完成更多事情。
en: With the development of computers, people expected to get more things done efficiently.

zh: 冷战期间，两位美国科学家提出了一个计算机“巨大网络”的构想。
en: During the Cold War, a "huge network" of computers was proposed by two American scientists.

zh: 通过计算机相互通信，它将使政府领导人能够彼此交流。
en: With computers talking to one another, it would enable government leaders to communicate with each other.

zh: 到20世纪60年代末，建立了一些小型网络，但只有少数计算机可以连接到它们。
en: By the end of the 1960s, some mini-networks were established, but only a few computers could connect to them.

zh: 1990年，蒂姆·伯纳斯-李发明了万维网，它使全世界的计算机能够相互通信。
en: In 1990, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web, which enabled computers all over the world to communicate with each other.

zh: 如今，对大多数人来说，没有互联网的生活是不可想象的。
en: Nowadays, life without the Internet for most people is unimaginable.

zh: 这些20世纪的先驱们都致力于提高地球上人类的生活质量。
en: These pioneers of the 20th century were all dedicated to improving the quality of human life on Earth.

zh: 这个星球上的人类生活已经转变为一个“地球村”，所有不同的国家都被共同的利益链连接在一起。
en: Human life on this planet has been transformed into a "global village", with all the different countries linked in the chain of common interests.

zh: 这一点毫无疑问。
en: There is no doubt about it.

zh: 如果没有这些科技先驱们的突破，无论是幸运的还是计划好的，我们今天所熟知的世界将会是一个完全不同的地方。
en: Without the breakthroughs of these pioneers in science and technology, whether lucky or planned, the world as we know it today would be a completely different place.

zh: 2018年3月14日，世界上最具影响力的科学家之一斯蒂芬·霍金在英格兰的家中去世，享年76岁。
en: On 14 March, 2018, one of the world's most influential scientists, Stephen Hawking, died at his home in England, aged 76.

zh: 霍金之所以著名，不仅因为他在理论物理学和宇宙学方面的杰出工作，还因为他克服了肌萎缩侧索硬化（一种影响肌肉控制的疾病）的挑战。
en: Hawking was famous, not just for his brilliant work in theoretical physics and cosmology, but also for overcoming the challenges of ALS (a disease that affects muscle control).

zh: 斯蒂芬·霍金于1942年1月8日出生于牛津。
en: Stephen Hawking was born on 8 January, 1942, in Oxford.

zh: 他于1959年进入牛津大学学习物理。
en: He went to the University of Oxford to study physics in 1959.

zh: 21岁时，当霍金在剑桥大学度过第一年时，医生发现他患有一种罕见的、缓慢进展的肌萎缩侧索硬化症。
en: At the age of 21, when Hawking spent his first year at the University of Cambridge, physicians discovered he had a rare, slow-progressing form of ALS.

zh: 他当时预计只能活几年。
en: He was only expected to live for a few years.

zh: 霍金后来在剑桥的天文研究所找到了一份工作。
en: Hawking later found a job at the Institute of Astronomy in Cambridge.

zh: 1985年，他不得不接受一次手术。
en: In 1985, he had to have an operation.

zh: 结果，他失去了说话的能力。
en: As a result, he lost his speech.

zh: 不久，他的医生开发了一种语音生成设备，使他能够说话。
en: Soon his doctors developed a piece of speech-generating equipment that allowed him to speak.

zh: 然而，随着他的病情恶化，他失去了双手的功能。
en: However, as his illness became worse, he lost the use of his hands.

zh: 尽管霍金的生理机能衰退了几十年，但他从未停止研究，并提出了许多关于黑洞的新想法。
en: Although Hawking's physical abilities declined over decades, he never ceased his studies and he developed a number of new ideas about black holes.

zh: 霍金认为，宇宙的诞生（"大爆炸"）创造了许多小黑洞。
en: Hawking believed that the birth of the universe (the "Big Bang") created many small black holes.

zh: 他的理论是，在黑洞的中心存在一种洞。
en: His theory was that there was a sort of hole in the centre of a black hole.

zh: 这个洞通向另一个宇宙，完全独立于我们的宇宙。
en: This hole led to another universe, completely separate from our own.

zh: 他1988年的著作《时间简史：从大爆炸到黑洞》在地球上每750人就售出一本。
en: His 1988 book, A Brief History of Time: From the Big Bang to Black Holes, sold one copy for every 750 people on Earth.

zh: 然而，许多人并不能真正理解他所写的内容。
en: However, many people could not really understand what he had written.

zh: 因此，霍金决定写一个更简单的版本，《时间更简史》（2005年）。
en: Therefore, Hawking decided to write a simpler version, A Briefer History of Time (2005).

zh: 在《大设计》（2010年，与伦纳德·姆洛迪诺合著）中，霍金主张我们应该寻找一种不同的方法来发现宇宙最深的奥秘。
en: In The Grand Design (2010, with Leonard Mlodinow), Hawking argued that we should look for a different way to discover the deepest secrets of the universe.

zh: 科学家们不应该试图找到一个全新的宏大解释，而应该将他们已有的所有想法整合起来。
en: Instead of trying to find one big new explanation, scientists should put together all the ideas that they already have.

zh: 在2012年伦敦奥运会开幕式上，霍金对九亿电视观众说："要仰望星空，不要低头看脚下......要保持好奇心。"
en: At the opening of the London 2012 Olympics, Hawking said to a TV audience of 900 million people: "Look up at the stars and not down at your feet ... be curious."

zh: 尽管身患疾病，霍金并不认为自己是一个不幸的人。
en: In spite of his disease, Hawking didn't consider himself an unlucky man.

zh: 他说疾病并没有阻止他组建家庭，并在工作中取得成功。
en: He said it had not prevented him from having a family, and being successful in his work.

zh: "当我21岁时，我的期望降到了零。"
en: "My expectations were reduced to zero when I was 21."

zh: "从那时起，一切都是额外的奖赏。"
en: "Everything since then has been a bonus."

zh: 并且他相信黑洞是太空中一个引力非常强的地方。
en: And he believed a black hole is a place in space where gravity is very strong.

zh: 任何掉进去的东西都无法出来。
en: Anything that falls into it cannot come out.

zh: 没有什么能从中逃脱，甚至连光也不能。
en: Nothing can escape from it, not even light.

zh: 因此，要看到黑洞是不可能的。
en: As a result, it is impossible to see a black hole.

zh: 詹姆斯·瓦特是一位工程师、科学家和发明家，1736年出生于苏格兰。
en: James Watt was an engineer, scientist and inventor, who was born in Scotland in 1736.

zh: 小时候，他在数学和科学领域就很有天赋。
en: As a child, he was gifted in the areas of maths and science.

zh: 在他12岁的一天，他正坐在家里的厨房里看着一壶烧开的水。
en: One day when he was 12 years old, he was sitting in his family kitchen watching a kettle boiling water.

zh: 水烧开时，蒸汽使得壶盖上下跳动。
en: As the water boiled, the steam made the top of the kettle bounce up and down.

zh: 他深入思考了正在发生的事情，因为蒸汽只是水，但似乎强大到足以掀起壶盖。
en: He thought deeply about what was happening, as steam was only water but seemed to be powerful enough to lift a kettle top.

zh: 他进一步反思了这个过程，估算着更大量的水能产生多大的力量。
en: He reflected further on the process, estimating how much more power could be created by a larger amount of water.

zh: 后来他开始用蒸汽做实验，并最终发明了蒸汽机。
en: Later he began to experiment with steam and eventually developed the steam engine.

zh: 这是对当时普遍使用的纽科门蒸汽机的一项重大改进。
en: This was a great improvement on the Newcomen steam engine, which was commonly used at the time.

zh: 瓦特的发明改变了人类历史的进程。
en: Watt's invention changed the course of human history.

zh: 蒸汽机为引发工业革命的工厂提供了动力。
en: Steam engines helped to power factories that started the Industrial Revolution.

zh: 随之而来的是新型的工作岗位以及能以更低成本获得的新产品。
en: With this came new types of jobs and the availability of new products at lower costs.

zh: 在交通运输领域，蒸汽机被用来为火车和船只提供动力，帮助人们在更短的时间内旅行更远的距离。
en: In the area of transportation, steam engines were used to power trains and boats, helping people to travel greater distances in much shorter times.

zh: 詹姆斯·瓦特是一位工程师、科学家和发明家，1736年出生于苏格兰。
en: James Watt was an engineer, scientist and inventor, who was born in Scotland in 1736.

zh: 有一天，当他12岁的时候，他正坐在家里的厨房里看着一壶水烧开。
en: One day when he was 12 years old, he was sitting in his family kitchen watching a kettle boiling water.

zh: 当水烧开时，蒸汽使壶盖上下跳动。
en: As the water boiled, the steam made the top of the kettle bounce up and down.

zh: 后来，他开始用蒸汽做实验，并最终发明了蒸汽机。
en: Later, he began to experiment with steam, and eventually developed a steam engine.

zh: 古代中国人与许多重要发明联系在一起，其中一些改变了世界，许多至今仍在使用。
en: The ancient Chinese are associated with many important inventions, some of which have changed the world, and many of which we still use today.

zh: 造纸术大约在2000年前被发明并开始使用。
en: Paper-making was invented and were using paper about 2,000 years ago.

zh: 中国早期的纸是由竹纤维制成的。
en: Early paper in China was made from bamboo fibre.

zh: 将植物放入水中，然后打成纸浆。
en: The plant was put in water and then beaten to a pulp.

zh: 然后将这种纸浆干燥成薄片。
en: This pulp was then dried into fine sheets.

zh: 由于纸的发明，中国成为最早开始制作历史记录、地图和文献的国家之一。
en: Because of the invention of paper, China was one of the first nations to start producing historical records, maps and literature.

zh: 火药是由中国人在公元八世纪发明的。
en: Gunpowder was invented by the Chinese in the eighth century AD.

zh: 早期的中国科学家发现了如何让东西爆炸。
en: Early Chinese scientists discovered how they could make something explode.

zh: 火药被用于战争，并导致了许多不同种类武器的发展。
en: Gunpowder was used in wars and led to the development of many different kinds of weapons.

zh: 例如，携带火药的火箭可以从竹管中发射向敌方士兵。
en: For example, rockets carrying gunpowder could be launched at enemy soldiers from a bamboo tube.

zh: 一位名叫毕升的中国工匠在公元1045年发明了活字印刷术。
en: A Chinese craftsman called Bi Sheng invented the movable-type printing in 1045 AD.

zh: 早期的印刷机是通过在木块上雕刻字符制成的。
en: Early printing presses were made by carving wooden blocks with characters.

zh: 然后将这些木块排列起来，形成页面上的文字。
en: The blocks were then arranged to form the words on a page.

zh: 这项发明使得复制书面作品更快、更容易，并导致了有史以来第一次书籍和文献的大规模生产。
en: This invention made it much quicker and easier to reproduce written work and led to the first ever mass-production of books and literature.

zh: 由于书籍更广泛地向公众提供，中国的普遍识字率比世界上任何其他地方都要高得多。
en: Because books became more widely available to the public, general literacy in China was a lot better than anywhere else in the world.

zh: 古代中国人注意到某些种类的金属通常排列成南北方向。
en: The ancient Chinese noticed that certain kinds of metals usually lined up in a north to south position.

zh: 到三世纪时，他们开始通过将小磁金属针浮在碗水中来制造第一个简单的指南针。
en: By the third century, they had started making the first simple compasses by floating small pins of magnetic metal in bowls of water.

zh: 到11世纪，中国人使用高度发达的指南针在海上导航他们的船只。
en: By the 11th century, the Chinese were using highly developed compasses to navigate their ships at sea.

zh: 在西方，直到15世纪才发现了磁力。
en: In the West, magnetism was not discovered until the 15th century.

zh: 直到这个时候，欧洲水手仍然通过星星导航。
en: Until this time, European sailors were still navigating by the stars.

zh: 世界上有许多发明改变了人们的生活。
en: There are many inventions in the world that have changed people's lives.

zh: 为了说明这一点，让我们看看三个简单的发明：时钟、冰箱和电梯。
en: To illustrate this, let's have a look at three simple inventions: the clock, the refrigerator and the elevator.

zh: 一个规范了我们几乎所做的一切，另一个影响了我们的饮食方式，最后一个则改变了我们城市的面貌。
en: One regulates almost everything we do, another affects the way we eat, and the last has changed the very look of our cities.

zh: 几个世纪以来，人们设计了多种不同的方式来测量时间的时钟。
en: Over the centuries, people have designed clocks that measure time in many different ways.

zh: 例如，古埃及人制造了一种时钟，它只是一个石头做的碗形容器，底部有一个洞。
en: The ancient Egyptians, for example, made a clock that was simply a stone, bowl-shaped container with a hole at the bottom.

zh: 容器里会装满水，水以恒定的速度从底部滴下。
en: The container would be filled with water which would drip from the bottom at a constant rate.

zh: 碗内侧的标记显示了已经过去了多少时间。
en: Markings on the inside of the bowl showed how much time had passed.

zh: 机械钟是一个相对较新的发明。
en: Mechanical clocks are a relatively new invention.

zh: 欧洲发明家在16和17世纪开发了第一批精确的机械钟。
en: European inventors developed the first accurate mechanical clocks in the 16th and 17th centuries.

zh: 这些钟表被设计用来以秒为单位测量时间。
en: These clocks and watches were designed to measure time in seconds.

zh: 在当今繁忙的现代世界中，我们日常生活的几乎每个部分都受到时间以及我们准确测量时间的能力的支配。
en: In today's busy and modern world, almost every part of our day-to-day lives is governed by time and our ability to measure it accurately.

zh: 如果没有手表或时钟，即使是赶火车、见朋友甚至煮鸡蛋这样简单的任务确实都会非常困难。
en: Without watches or clocks, even simple tasks like catching a train, meeting a friend or even boiling an egg would be very difficult indeed.

zh: 时间可能支配着我们的生活，但食物让我们得以生存。
en: Time may govern our lives, but food keeps us alive.

zh: 因此，保存食物一直是人们的一个重要考虑。
en: Therefore, preserving food has always been an important consideration for people.

zh: 几千年前，人们意识到可以通过将肉埋在雪中来保存它。
en: Thousands of years ago, people realised that they could preserve meat by burying it in snow.

zh: 中国人是最早开始专门为保存食物而收集和储存冰的人群之一。
en: The Chinese were among the first people to begin collecting and storing ice specifically for the purpose of preserving food.

zh: 早期的冰箱实际上是冰盒——装满冰的容器。
en: Early refrigerators were actually ice-boxes — containers filled with ice.

zh: 这种方法需要每天将新鲜的冰送到家里。
en: This method required fresh ice to be delivered to homes daily.

zh: 我们今天所知的冰箱工作方式非常不同：它们使用气体来冷却容器内部。
en: Refrigerators as we know them today work very differently: they use gases to cool the inside of the container.

zh: 奥利弗·埃文斯在1805年发明了第一台制冷机。
en: Oliver Evans invented the first refrigeration machine in 1805.

zh: 现代冰箱的工作原理与埃文斯的非常相似。
en: Modern refrigerators work much the same way as Evans'.

zh: 原始设计。冰箱金属线圈内的压缩气体在膨胀时，会吸收周围空气中的热量。
en: original. Compressed gases in a metal coil inside the refrigerator suck the heat out of the surrounding air as the gases expand.

zh: 在中国，20年前，冰箱是人们为了改善生活而购买的“三大件”之一。
en: In China, 20 years ago, refrigerators were on the list of "Three New Big Things" for people to buy to improve their lives.

zh: 如今，它已成为大多数家庭的常见物品，人们很难想象没有它的生活。
en: Now it is a common object in most homes and people would find it difficult to imagine living without it.

zh: 与冰箱不同，电梯通常不在人们的家中，但它们帮助改变了数百万人的生活环境。
en: Unlike refrigerators, elevators are generally not found in people's homes, but they have helped change the environment that millions of people live in.

zh: 来自古代文明的科学家和工程师使用绳索来提升重物，但直到1857年，一位名叫伊莱沙·奥的斯的人才设计并制造了一种使用液压系统在建筑物楼层之间运送人的机器。
en: Scientists and engineers from ancient civilisations used ropes to lift heavy objects, but it wasn't until 1857 that a man called Elisha Otis designed and built a machine that used hydraulics to carry people between the floors of a building.

zh: 1880年，德国发明家维尔纳·冯·西门子推出了第一台电动电梯——我们今天所知的电梯就此诞生。
en: In 1880, German inventor, Werner von Siemens, introduced the first electric powered elevator — and the lift, as we know it today, was born.

zh: 电梯不仅通过使人们能够居住在更大更高的建筑中而改变了我们的生活，也改变了城市的天际线。
en: Elevators have changed not only our lives by making it possible to live in bigger and taller buildings, but also the city skylines.

zh: 如果没有发明电梯，像北京、上海和香港这样的现代中国城市看起来将会非常不同。
en: Modern Chinese cities like Beijing, Shanghai and Hong Kong would look very different indeed if the elevator hadn't been invented.

zh: 下次当你依赖这些发明中的任何一个时，要心怀感激，并思考其中所投入的科学和想象力，以及它们如何极大地改变了我们的日常生活。
en: Next time you rely on any of these inventions, be grateful and think about the science and imagination that have gone into them and how much they have changed our everyday life.

zh: 每一个伟大的想法都需要一个拥有非凡远见并愿意成为先驱的人。
en: Every great idea needs someone who has an incredible vision and is willing to be a pioneer.

zh: 这类人会去无人去过的地方，尝试无人尝试过的事情。
en: This type of person will go where no one has gone before, and try things no one has tried before.

zh: 他们有决心要证明的理论，并将为此不懈努力。
en: They have theory that they are determined to prove and will work tirelessly to do so.

zh: 许多最优秀的发明家都受到改善人类生活质量的愿望的启发，无论是为了现在还是为了子孙后代。
en: Many of the best inventors have been inspired by a desire to improve the quality of human life, both for now and for future generations.

zh: 一些科学家毕生致力于寻找治疗癌症等疾病的方法。
en: Some scientists have dedicated their careers to finding cures for illnesses such as cancer.

zh: 像生物学家这样的人帮助我们理解自然世界，无论是可见的还是不可见的。
en: Others like biologist help us understand the natural world, both seen and unseen.

zh: 物理学家帮助我们理解物质、能量和我们宇宙的基本原理。
en: Physicist help us understand matter, energy and the basic principles of our universe.

zh: 工程师致力于开发节省劳力的发明，并以极高的精度将卫星和人送入太空。
en: Engineers work on developing labour-saving inventions and launch satellites and people into space with great accuracy.

zh: 100年前，人们能想象到会有一个国际空间站环绕地球运行吗？
en: 100 years ago, could people have imagined that there would be an International Space Station orbit the Earth?

zh: 我们确实已经走了这么远！
en: We have really come so far!

zh: 因此，我们需要继续投资于培养年轻人的科学素养，以确保为子孙后代、我们的环境和世界带来更积极的成果。
en: For that reason, we need to continue to invest in training young people in science to ensure more positive outcome for future generations, our environment and the world.

zh: 有如此多杰出的科学家和科学发现，很难选择最重要的一个。
en: There are so many amazing scientists and scientific discoveries that it is hard to choose the most important one.

zh: 20世纪涌现的最杰出的科学家之一是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦。
en: One of the premier scientists to emerge in the 20th century was Albert Einstein.

zh: 他向我们展示了质量、能量和时间不是恒定的，这彻底改变了我们对宇宙的理解。
en: He showed us that mass, energy and time are not constant, which changed our entire understanding of the universe.

zh: 这被称为“相对论”。
en: This was known as the "theory of relativity".

zh: 爱因斯坦改变了我们对自身和周围宇宙的理解，但我认为，是亚历山大·弗莱明改变了我们的生活质量。
en: Einstein changed our understanding of ourselves and the universe around us, but it was Alexander Fleming who, I think, changed the quality of our lives.

zh: 他发现蓝色霉菌（一种青霉素）可以杀死细菌并拯救全世界数百万人。
en: His discovery that blue mould a form of penicillin could kill bacteria and save millions of people around the world.

zh: 曾经致命的疾病现在变得微不足道，多亏了这种神奇的药物的使用。
en: Illnesses that used to be fatal are now minor thanks to the use of this amazing drug.

zh: 最后，第一台计算机和互联网的发明产生了深远的影响，并彻底改变了世界。
en: Finally, the invention of the first computer and the Internet has had far-reaching effects and changed the world entirely.
