zh: 最初，联网计算机相互通信的想法是由美国科学家在冷战期间提出的。
en: Originally, the idea of networked computers communicating with each other was proposed by American scientists during the Cold War.

zh: 直到1990年，蒂姆·伯纳斯-李才发明了万维网。
en: It wasn't until 1990 that Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web.

zh: 从那时起，连接性变得如此普遍，以至于甚至一岁的孩子都能够点击和滑动智能手机屏幕，打开一个内容的世界。
en: Since then, connectivity has become so commonplace that even a one-year-old child is able to tap and slide a screen on a smartphone and open up a world of content.

zh: 我们现在可以即时、方便地工作、生活和交流。
en: We can now work, live and communicate instantly and conveniently.

zh: 让我们看看什么将被评为21世纪唯一最重要的发现。
en: Let's see what will be rated as the single most important discovery of the 21st century.

zh: 主持人：嗯，是的。我想是的。他去上学，然后他有家庭作业、私人课程、体育和音乐。
en: Presenter: Umm, yes. I suppose so. He goes to school, and then he has homework, private lessons, sport, and music.

zh: 邦德博士：没错。这个清单还在继续。
en: Dr. Bond: Exactly. The list goes on and on.

zh: 主持人：拥有如此忙碌的生活不一定是件好事。
en: Presenter: And having a very busy life like this is not necessarily a good thing.

zh: 邦德博士：不，不是！如果你没有时间停下来、思考和做白日梦，你就永远不会有一个“顿悟时刻”。
en: Dr. Bond: No, it's not! If you have no time to stop, think and daydream, you'll never have an "aha moment".

zh: 主持人：能再多说一点吗？我们如何鼓励创新和创造力？
en: Presenter: A bit more about that? How can we encourage innovation and creativity?

zh: 邦德博士：这很简单。去散散步，听些音乐。让你的大脑休息一会儿。那时伟大的想法就会出现在你脑海中。
en: Dr. Bond: It's simple. Go for a walk and listen to some music. Switch your brain off for a while. That's when great ideas will come to you.

zh: 科幻小说是一种以科学知识为基础进行想象性创作的体裁。
en: Science fiction is a genre in which scientific knowledge forms a basis for imaginative fiction.

zh: 19世纪的法国作家儒勒·凡尔纳常被视为科幻小说之父。
en: The 19th century French writer, Jules Verne, is often seen as the father of science fiction.

zh: 他运用自己的工程学知识创作了关于月球或海底旅行的故事（《海底两万里》）。
en: He used his knowledge of engineering to write stories about journeys to the moon or under the sea (20,000 Leagues Under the Sea).

zh: 从20世纪初开始，科幻小说开始流行起来。
en: From the beginning of the 20th century, science fiction started to become popular.

zh: 严肃文学作家也开始对这一体裁产生兴趣，例如奥尔德斯·赫胥黎及其对未来生活的深刻描述（《美丽新世界》）。
en: Serious authors also began to be interested in the genre, such as Aldous Huxley with his perceptive account of life in the future (Brave New World).

zh: 本世纪中叶，科幻小说的黄金时代开始了，涌现出了以科学家艾萨克·阿西莫夫、阿瑟·C·克拉克和雷·布拉德伯里为代表的杰出作家。
en: In the middle of the century, a golden age for science fiction began with outstanding writers such as the scientist Isaac Asimov, Arthur C. Clarke and Ray Bradbury.

zh: 他们的故事不仅着眼于未来的生活，还探讨了人类可能的命运。
en: Their stories not only looked at life in the future, but also the possible destiny of the human race.

zh: 雷·布拉德伯里1920年出生于美国。
en: Ray Bradbury was born in the USA in 1920.

zh: 他最著名的小说是《火星编年史》，描述了地球人对火星的殖民，以及《华氏451度》，设定在一个禁止阅读的未来世界。
en: His most famous novels are The Martian Chronicles, which describes the colonisation of Mars by Earth men, and Fahrenheit 451, set in a future world where reading is forbidden.

zh: 他们在火星上一个空海的边缘，沙地上，有一座水晶柱子的房子。
en: They had a house of crystal pillars on the planet Mars by the edge of an empty sea, upon the sand.

zh: 每天早晨，你都能看到K太太吃着从水晶墙上长出的金色果实，或者用一把把磁性灰尘打扫房子，这些灰尘带着所有污垢，被热风吹走。
en: And every morning you could see Mrs K eating the golden fruits that grew from the crystal walls, or cleaning the house with handfuls of magnetic dust which, taking all dirt with it, blew away on the hot wind.

zh: 下午，当化石海温暖而静止，酒树在院子里僵硬地站立着，小小的火星骨头镇完全封闭，没有人飘出门外，你可以看到K先生在他的房间里自娱自乐，阅读一本带有凸起象形文字的金属书，他的手在上面轻抚，就像弹奏竖琴一样。
en: Afternoons, when the fossil sea was warm and motionless, and the wine trees stood stiff in the yard, and the little Martian bone town was all enclosed, and no one drifted out their doors, you could see Mr K playing himself in his room, reading from a metal book with raised hieroglyphs over which he brushed his hand, as one might play a harp.

zh: 随着他的手指抚摸，书中传出一个声音，一个柔和而古老的声音，讲述着当海洋在岸边是红色蒸汽以及古人带着金属昆虫和电蜘蛛云团参加战斗时的故事。
en: And from the book, as his fingers stroked, a voice sang, a soft ancient voice, which told tales of when the sea was red steam on the shore and ancient men had carried clouds of metal insects and electric spiders into battle.

zh: K先生和K太太在死海边生活了20年，他们的祖先也住在同一所房子里，这所房子像花一样转动并跟随太阳，已有十个世纪。
en: Mr and Mrs K had lived by the dead sea for 20 years and their ancestors had lived in the same house, which turned and followed the sun, flower-like, for ten centuries.

zh: K先生和K太太并不老。
en: Mr and Mrs K were not old.

zh: 他们有着真正火星人白皙、略带棕色的皮肤，黄色的硬币般的眼睛，柔和悦耳的声音。
en: They had the fair, brownish skin of the true Martian, the yellow coin eyes, the soft musical voices.

zh: 他们曾经喜欢用化学火焰画画，在酒树用绿色液体填满运河的季节里游泳，以及在谈话室里蓝色的磷光肖像旁一起聊天直到黎明。
en: Once they had liked painting pictures with chemical fire, swimming in the canals in the seasons when the wine trees filled them with green liquors, and talking into the dawn together by the blue phosphorous portraits in the speaking-room.

zh: 他们现在不快乐。
en: They were not happy now.

zh: 今天早上，K太太站在柱子之间，听着沙漠沙子发热，融化成黄色的蜡，似乎在地平线上流动。
en: This morning Mrs K stood between the pillars, listening to the desert sands heat, melt into yellow wax, and seemingly run on the horizon.

zh: 有大事要发生了。
en: Something great was going to happen.

zh: 等累了，她穿过雾气缭绕的柱子。
en: Tired of waiting, she walked through the misting pillars.

zh: 一阵细雨从有凹槽的柱顶涌出，冷却了灼热的空气，轻轻落在她身上。
en: A gentle rain sprang from the fluted pillar-tops, cooling the scorching air, falling gently on her.

zh: 在炎热的日子里，这就像在小溪中行走。
en: On hot days, it was like walking in a creek.

zh: 房子的地板因凉爽的溪流而闪闪发光。
en: The floors of the house glittered with cool streams.

zh: 在远处，她听到丈夫稳定地演奏着他的书，他的手指从不厌倦那些老歌。
en: In the distance she heard her husband playing his book steadily, his fingers never tired of the old songs.

zh: 她静静地希望，有一天他能再次像对待他那些不可思议的书一样，花同样多的时间拥抱和抚摸她，就像对待一把小竖琴。
en: Quietly she wished he might one day again spend as much time holding and touching her like a little harp as he did to his incredible books.

zh: 她摇了摇头，一个难以察觉的、宽恕的耸肩。
en: She shook her head, an imperceptible, forgiving shrug.

zh: 她的眼睑轻轻地合在她金色的眼睛上。
en: Her eyelids closed softly down upon her golden eyes.

zh: 婚姻使人在还年轻的时候就变得衰老和熟悉。
en: Marriage made people old and familiar, while still young.

zh: 她躺在一把椅子里，这把椅子甚至会随着她的移动而改变形状以适应她。
en: She lay back in a chair that moved to take her shape even as she moved.

zh: 她紧张地紧紧闭上眼睛。
en: She closed her eyes tightly and nervously.

zh: 她棕色的手指颤抖着，抬起来，抓向空中。
en: Her brown fingers trembled, came up, grasped at the air.

zh: 片刻之后，她坐了起来， startled, gasping.
en: A moment later she sat up, startled, gasping.

zh: 她迅速环顾四周，仿佛期待有人在她面前。
en: She glanced about swiftly, as if expecting someone there before her.

zh: 她似乎很失望；柱子之间的空间是空的。
en: She seemed disappointed; the space between the pillars was empty.

zh: 她的丈夫出现在一扇三角形的门里。
en: Her husband appeared in a triangular door.

zh: "你叫我了吗？"他烦躁地问道。
en: "Did you call?" he asked irritably.

zh: "我以为我听到你叫喊。"
en: "I thought I heard you cry out."

zh: "我叫了吗？我几乎睡着了，做了一个梦！"
en: "Did I? I was almost asleep and had a dream!"

zh: "在白天？你不常那样做。"
en: "In the daytime? You don't often do that."

zh: 她坐着，仿佛被梦击中了脸。
en: She sat as if struck in the face by the dream.

zh: "多么奇怪，非常奇怪，"她喃喃地说。
en: "How strange, how very strange," she murmured.

zh: “哦？”他显然想回到他的书上去。
en: "Oh?" He evidently wished to return to his book.

zh: 多么奇怪的男人，然而——相当英俊。”
en: What a strange man, and yet — quite handsome."

zh: “我梦见了一个男人。”
en: "I dreamt about a man."

zh: “一个高个子男人，六英尺一英寸高。”
en: "A tall man, six-foot one-inch tall."

zh: “多么荒谬；一个巨人，一个畸形的巨人。”
en: "How absurd; a giant, a misshapen giant."

zh: “不知怎的——他看起来还不错。
en: "Somehow — he looked all right.

zh: 尽管个子很高。
en: In spite of being tall.

zh: 而且他有——哦，我知道你会觉得这很傻——他有蓝眼睛！”
en: And he had — oh, I know you'll think it silly— he had blue eyes!"

zh: “蓝眼睛！天哪！”K先生喊道。
en: "Blue eyes! Gods!" cried Mr K.

zh: 我猜他有黑头发？”
en: I suppose he had black hair?"

zh: “你怎么猜到的？”她很兴奋。
en: "How did you guess?" She was excited.

zh: “我选了一个最不可能的颜色，”他冷冷地回答。
en: "I picked the most unlikely colour," he replied coldly.

zh: “嗯，就是黑色的！”她喊道。
en: "Well black it was!" she cried.

zh: “而且他的皮肤非常白；哦，他太不寻常了！
en: "And he had a very white skin; oh, he was most unusual!

zh: 他穿着一件奇怪的制服，他从天而降，愉快地对我说话。”
en: He was dressed in a strange uniform and he came down out of the sky and spoke pleasantly to me."

zh: “从天而降；真是胡说八道！”
en: "Out of the sky; what nonsense!"

zh: “他坐在一个明亮的金属物体里，在阳光下闪闪发光，”她回忆道。
en: "He came in a bright metal thing that glittered in the sun," she remembered.

zh: 她闭上眼睛再次塑造它。
en: She closed her eyes to shape it again.

zh: “我梦见了天空，有东西闪闪发光——就像一枚硬币抛向空中，突然它变大了，轻轻落下着陆，一个长长的银色飞行器，圆形的，很陌生。
en: "I dreamt there was the sky and something sparkled — like a coin thrown into the air, and suddenly it grew large and fell down softly to land, a long silver craft, round and alien.

zh: 银色物体的侧面一扇门打开了，这个高个子男人走了出来。”
en: And a door opened in the side of the silver object and this tall man stepped out."

zh: “如果你工作更努力，你就不会做这些愚蠢的梦了。”
en: "If you worked harder, you wouldn't have these silly dreams."

zh: “我挺享受的，”她回答道，向后靠着。
en: "I rather enjoyed it," she replied, lying back.

zh: “我从未怀疑过自己有这样的想象力。
en: "I never suspected myself of such imagination.

zh: 黑头发，蓝眼睛，白皮肤！
en: Black hair, blue eyes, and white skin!

zh: 多么奇怪的男人，然而——相当英俊。”
en: What a strange man, and yet — quite handsome."

zh: 我不是故意想出他的；他只是在我打盹时出现在我的脑海里。
en: I didn't think him up on purpose; he just came in my mind while I drowsed.

zh: 那不像一个梦。
en: It wasn't like a dream.

zh: 它是如此出乎意料和不同。
en: It was so unexpected and different.

zh: 他看着我说：‘我乘我的飞船来自第三颗行星。
en: He looked at me and said, 'I've come from the third planet in my ship.

zh: 我的名字是纳撒尼尔·约克。’”
en: My name is Nathaniel York.'"

zh: “一个愚蠢的名字；这根本不算名字，”丈夫反对道。
en: "A stupid name; it's no name at all," objected the husband.

zh: “当然很愚蠢，因为这是一个梦，”她轻声解释道。
en: "Of course it's stupid, because it's a dream," she explained softly.

zh: “他说，这是第一次穿越太空的旅行。
en: "And he said, This is the first trip across space.

zh: 我们的飞船里只有两个人，我和我的朋友伯特。’”
en: There are only two of us in our ship, myself and my friend Bert.'"

zh: “他说，‘我们来自地球；那是我们星球的名字，’K夫人继续说道。
en: "And he said, 'We're from the Earth; that's the name of our planet,' continued Mrs K.

zh: 而且他使用了另一种语言。
en: And he used another language.

zh: 她用一句话和一只手放在他的胳膊上阻止了他。
en: She stopped him with a word and a hand on his arm.

zh: “你有没有想过——嗯，如果第三颗行星上有人居住？”
en: "Do you ever wonder if — well, if there are people living on the third planet?"

zh: “第三颗行星无法维持生命，”丈夫耐心地陈述道。
en: "The third planet is incapable of supporting life" stated the husband patiently.

zh: “我们的科学家说过，大气中的氧气太多了。”
en: "Our scientists have said there's far too much oxygen in the atmosphere."

zh: “但是如果有人，难道不会很迷人吗？
en: "But wouldn't it be fascinating if there were people?

zh: 而且他们乘坐某种飞船穿越太空？”
en: And they travelled through space in some sort of ship?"

zh: “真的，伊拉，你知道我多么讨厌这种情绪化的哀嚎。
en: "Really, Ylla, you know how I hate this emotional wailing.

zh: 让我们继续我们的工作吧。”
en: Let's get on with our work."

zh: 你听说过“六度分隔”这个短语吗？
en: Have you heard the phrase "Six Degrees of Separation"?

zh: 这个观点认为，我们通过大约六个人就可以认识世界上所有其他人。
en: It is the idea that we all know everyone else in the world through about six people.

zh: 我最近订阅了一份双月刊通讯，它在每月的第一个和最后一个星期五通过电子邮件发送。
en: I recently signed up to a bimonthly newsletter that is emailed on the first and last Friday of every month.

zh: 它有很多关于著名实验的有趣文章。
en: It has lots of interesting articles on famous experiments.

zh: 在最新的通讯中，他们决定尝试重现最初证明我们所有人都拥有这个庞大的人际网络理论的实验。
en: In the latest newsletter, they decided to try to recreate the experiment that originally proved the theory of how we all have this large network of people.

zh: 他们给一组随机的人发了电子邮件，并邀请他们参与。
en: They emailed a random group of people and asked them to participate.

zh: 然后，这个小样本的人群收到了一个包裹，要求他们通过发送给他们认识的人来传递给一个陌生人。
en: This small sample of people were then sent a package to pass on to a stranger by sending it to someone they knew.

zh: 他们把它发送给另一个人，然后那个人又发送给他或她认识的人，从而开始了一个链条。
en: They sent it along to someone who then sent it to a person he or she knew, starting a chain.

zh: 在下一期中，通讯将公布结果。
en: In the next issue, the newsletter will release the results.

zh: 我迫不及待地想看看这个理论是否被证实！
en: I can't wait to see if the theory is proved!

zh: 我们过去住在市中心，在那里快乐地生活了很多年。
en: We used to live in the city centre and were happy there for many years.

zh: 大多数家庭都是由像我们这样的年轻家庭组成的。
en: Most of the households were made up of young families like ours.

zh: 人们之间有着紧密的联系；每个人都互相关心，感到安全。
en: There was a strong bond between people; everyone cared for each other and felt safe.

zh: 我会说我们有惊人的社区精神。
en: I would say we had amazing community spirit.

zh: 我经常听到其他人说他们羡慕我们，并希望住在我们的社区。
en: I often heard from other people that they envied us and wished they lived in our neighbourhood.

zh: 然而，这一切在过去五年左右的时间里发生了变化。
en: However, that all changed in the past five years or so.

zh: 城市削减了我们地区的警察数量，因此不幸的是，犯罪率上升了。
en: The city cut back on the number of police officers in our area so unfortunately, the crime rate increased.

zh: 因此，许多有能力的人离开了，去了其他地区。
en: As a consequence of this, many people who could afford to, left for other areas.

zh: 我们要求地方议会提供额外的警察，但他们说他们不能。
en: We asked the local council to provide additional police but they said they could not.

zh: 经常发生入室盗窃和汽车盗窃，而且每个月都变得更糟。
en: There were break-ins and car thefts frequently and it just got worse every month.

zh: 可悲的是，情况变得如此糟糕，以至于我们不得不卖掉房子搬到郊区。
en: Sadly, things got so bad that we had to sell our house and move to the suburb.

zh: 这里每个人都很友好，人与人之间没有冲突。
en: Everyone is nice here and there is no conflict between people.

zh: 尽管如此，我仍然怀念过去的日子。
en: Still, I miss the old days.

zh: 当我还是个小男孩时，我失去了我的母亲和父亲。
en: When I was a young boy, I lost my mother and father.

zh: 作为一个没有父母的人，生活对我来说很艰难，我常常感到孤独。
en: As a person without a father or mother life was difficult for me and I was often lonely.

zh: 我和其他孩子住在一个家里，我的卧室空荡荡的，几乎没有玩具或可以享受的东西。
en: I lived in a home with other children and my bedroom was empty with few toys or things to enjoy.

zh: 虽然我最终结了婚，有了自己的孩子，但一想到我的父母，我总是感到悲伤。
en: Although I married eventually and had my own children, I always felt sad thinking about my parents.

zh: 有一天，我收到了一封邮寄来的信。
en: One day I got a letter in the post.

zh: 信上说它来自我的姐姐！
en: It said it was from my sister!

zh: 读到这个我很震惊，因为从来没有人告诉过我我还有其他的家庭成员。
en: I was shocked to read this and as it had never been told to me that I had other family members.

zh: 信中说我的姐姐想见我，并告诉了我见面的时间和地点。
en: The letter said my sister would like to meet me and told me the time and place to go.

zh: 在我见到她的那天，我到了她的家。
en: On the day I met her, I arrived at her home.

zh: 那是一栋漂亮的旧房子，有蓝色的墙壁和白色的窗帘。
en: It was a beautiful old house with blue walls and white window coverings.

zh: 当我走上花园小径时，门被一个看起来和我母亲一模一样的女人猛地打开了！
en: As I went up the garden path, the door was flung open by a woman who looked just like my mother!

zh: 起初我震惊得呆在原地，但她跳进了我的怀里，我们拥抱在一起。
en: At first I was stuck in one place with shock but she jumped into my arms and we hugged.

zh: 这是一个如此特殊的时刻，我发现自己高兴得哭了起来。
en: It was such a special moment that I found myself crying with joy.

zh: 我的姐姐说她也不知道自己有一个弟弟，但她在过去的一年里查阅了我们的家族历史，并发现了我的名字。
en: My sister said she too was unaware that she had a brother but that she had looked into our family history in the past year and found my name.

zh: 知道我有一个姐姐给了我很大的安慰，因为我和过去有了联系。
en: Knowing that I have a sister gives me so much comfort as I have a connection to the past.

zh: 嗯，我喜欢炸鸡，所以我想我肯定会喜欢那个。
en: Well, I like fried chicken, so I guess I would definitely like that.

zh: 但是印度人不也吃卷心菜和姜之类的东西吗？
en: But don't Indians also eat cabbage and ginger thing?

zh: 卷心菜和姜之类的东西？你是指泡菜吗？
en: Cabbage and ginger thing? Do you mean kimchi?

zh: 是的，它很辣，不是吗？
en: Yes, it's hot, isn't it?

zh: 是的，你说得对，但泡菜不是印度菜。
en: Yes, you're right, but kimchi isn't an Indian dish.

zh: 在回答问题之前，我们将探讨支持和反对使用社交媒体的论点。
en: We will look at the arguments for and against using social media before answering the questions.

zh: 支持社交媒体的论点声称，它可以通过促进与不同地理区域人们的关系来加强社会纽带。
en: An argument for social media claims it can strengthen social bonds by facilitating relationships with people in different geographical areas.

zh: 例如，社交媒体帮助了多年没有联系的老校友重新取得联系。
en: For example, social media has helped old school friends to get back in touch after many years without contact.

zh: 人们现在通过社交媒体获得工作或为他们的学习研究信息。
en: People secure jobs now through social media or research information for their studies.

zh: 这些是使用社交媒体的真正好处。
en: These are real benefits of using social media.

zh: 在我看来，社交媒体的缺点超过了其好处。
en: In my opinion, the disadvantages of social media outweigh the benefits.

zh: 许多人使用的论点是，社交媒体上的互动不能替代面对面的交流。
en: The argument used by many people is that interactions on social media are no substitute for face-to-face communication.

zh: 例如，当你和朋友外出时，他们有多少次会查看手机。
en: When you are out with friends how many times do they check their phones, for example.

zh: 人们太容易沉迷于这种在线生活，而不是在现实生活中交流。
en: It is all too easy to become absorbed in this online life rather than communicate in real life.

zh: 研究强调，在现实生活中 physically 大声笑出来远比用打字表达的“哈哈”或“LOL”来替代这一行为更令人满足。
en: Research highlights it is far more satisfying to physically laugh out loud in real life rather than to replace the act with a typed representation of laughter such as "haha" or "LOL".

zh: 理想情况下，我们应该有足够的自控力来限制我们对社交媒体的使用，同时享受它可能带来的好处。
en: Ideally we should have enough self-control to be able to limit our use of social media, enjoying the benefits it can bring.

zh: 我认为我们应该努力做到这一点，尽管在当今这个互联的世界里，这么多人在网上生活，这不是一件容易的事。
en: I think we should aim to do this, although it is not an easy job in today's connected world where so many people live their lives online.

zh: 无论是在商业还是个人生活中，我总是鼓励人们保持冷静和克制的本性，不要陷入大争吵或互相喊叫。
en: Both in business and in personal lives, I always encourage people to have a calm and controlled nature and not get into big arguments or shout at each other.

zh: 相反，我鼓励他们利用自己的经验和智慧，清晰地沟通他们的感受和期望。
en: Instead, I encourage them to use their experience and wisdom and communicate their feelings and expectations clearly.

zh: 当双方都这样做时，我们可以制定一个计划来克服他们的冲突。
en: When both sides do this, we can develop a plan to overcome their conflict.

zh: 我密切观察双方，确保他们正在贯彻执行他们的行动，而且通常我发现即使是看似复杂的事情也可以被克服。
en: I observe both sides closely to make sure they are following through with their actions and, usually I find that even things that seem complicated can be overcome.

zh: 当战斗在他周围激烈进行时，将军向他的士兵们大喊，让他们向战斗的前线靠近。
en: As the battle raged around him, the general shouted to his soldiers to move closer to the frontier of the battle.

zh: 当他们到达树林中的一片空地时，他们对所发现的一切感到震惊。
en: When they reached a clearing in the trees they were shocked at what they found.

zh: 大约有50名各个年龄段的平民，从年幼的孩子到年迈的男人和女人。
en: There were approximately 50 civilians of all ages, from young children to old men and women.

zh: 他们眼中流露出恐慌的神色，许多年幼的孩子在哭泣。
en: They had a look of panic in their eyes and many of the younger ones were weeping.

zh: 许多年长的人似乎在祈祷。
en: Many of the older people seemed to be praying.

zh: 一位妇女站起来对他们说话，作为这个群体的代表。
en: One woman stood to speak to them, acting as a representative of the group.

zh: 她说炸弹被投到了他们的村庄，许多人已经死亡。
en: She said bombs had been dropped on their village and many people had died.

zh: 那些幸存者在这里避难。
en: Those who survived had taken shelter here.

zh: 她警告将军敌人就在附近，事实上，他们能听到附近机关枪的声音。
en: She warned the general that the enemy was near, In fact, they could hear the noise of the machine guns nearby.

zh: 将军对这位妇女提供的信息表示感谢。
en: The general expressed his appreciation to the woman for the information.

zh: 突然附近传来一声可怕的声音，地面震动。
en: Suddenly there was a terrible sound nearby and the ground shook.

zh: 将军命令他的士兵们尽快将人们转移到安全地带。
en: The general ordered his soldiers to get the people to safety as quickly as possible.

zh: 我的名字是纳西莱莱，我来自赞比亚。
en: My name is Nasilele and I am from Zambia.

zh: 在过去的两年里，我一直在中非共和国担任联合国维和人员。
en: I've been working as a UN Peacekeeper in the Central African Republic for the past two years.

zh: 在此之前，我是我国军队的一名飞行员，我非常喜欢我的工作。
en: Before this, I was a pilot in my country's army and I enjoyed my work a lot.

zh: 然而，赞比亚是一个非常和平的国家，我知道非洲还有其他一些国家，那里发生了很多暴力冲突。
en: However, Zambia is a very peaceful country and I was aware that there are other countries in Africa, where there has been a lot of violent conflict.

zh: 我觉得帮助那些陷入这些冲突中的无辜人民是我的责任，所以我报名加入了联合国。
en: I felt it was my duty to help innocent people who are caught in the middle of these conflicts, so I signed up with the United Nations.

zh: 中非共和国是一个相对较小的国家，拥有五百万人口。
en: The Central African Republic is a relatively small country of five million people.

zh: 自从20世纪50年代从法国独立以来，政府和不希望领导人掌权的叛军之间发生了许多暴力冲突。
en: Since it got independence from France in the 1950s, there have been many violent conflicts between the government and rebels who do not want the leaders in power.

zh: 军队经常与领导人发生冲突，这导致了许多
en: Often the army has been in conflict with leaders, which has led to many

zh: 问题。2012年，一场内战开始了。不同宗教和种族群体之间的冲突是主要原因。
en: problems. In 2012, a civil war began. Clashes between different religions and ethnic groups were the main causes.

zh: 由于这场内战，超过一百万人被迫逃离家园，并有许多人死亡和受伤。
en: Due to this civil war, over one million people were forced to flee their homes and there were many deaths and injuries.

zh: 作为回应，2014年，联合国在该国设立了一个维和特派团。
en: In response to this, in 2014, the United Nations established a peacekeeping mission in the country.

zh: 我的维和同事来自许多不同的国家，包括巴基斯坦、孟加拉国、埃及、卢旺达等。
en: My fellow peacekeepers are from many different countries including Pakistan, Bangladesh, Egypt, Rwanda and others.

zh: 这是一件好事，因为它确保了许多不同的文化和背景都在为相同的目标而努力。
en: This is a good thing as it ensures that there are many different cultures and backgrounds all working towards the same goals.

zh: 我们的角色随着每天不同的任务而变化。
en: Our roles vary with many different tasks from one day to the next.

zh: 我们监测城市和村庄以确保没有冲突发生，帮助将食物和药品运送给需要的人，并为我们所做和所见的一切撰写报告。
en: We monitor cities and villages to ensure that no conflict is occurring, help bring supplies of food and medicine to those who need it and write reports on everything we do and see.

zh: 我们的角色从不涉及针对他人的暴力。
en: Our role never involves violence against others.

zh: 我们是一道屏障，可以将冲突中的人们分开。
en: We are a barrier that can keep people in conflict apart.

zh: 作为一名女性，我特别受到联合国维和人员的重视，因为我可以成为该国许多妇女和女孩的榜样。
en: As a woman, I am particularly valued by the United Nations Peacekeepers as I can act as a role model for many women and girls in the country.

zh: 当她们看到我的角色以及别人如何尊重我时，我希望她们也觉得自己值得被尊重并与男性平等对待。
en: When they see my role and how others respect me, I hope they feel that they also deserve to be respected and treated equally to men.

zh: 我的生活一点也不光鲜！
en: My life is not glamorous in any way!

zh: 工作时间长，环境非常艰苦。
en: It's long hours, in a very tough environment.

zh: 我的住宿条件基本，只有一个共享房间里的床，以及一个小厨房和浴室。
en: I have basic accommodation with just a bed in a shared room and a small kitchen and bathroom.

zh: 当然，远离家人和朋友有时也非常艰难。
en: Of course, being away from my family and friends is also very hard at times.

zh: 然而，我知道由于联合国维和人员的存在，中非共和国更安全了，这让一切都变得值得。
en: However, I know that the Central African Republic is safer because of the UN Peacekeepers, and that makes it all worthwhile.

zh: 多个国家就探索火星的计划发表了一份联合声明。
en: A collective statement has been issued by a number of countries regarding plans to explore the planet Mars.

zh: 此前，一种能够传输图片和信号的机器已被发射出去，以探索该星球的地貌和化学构成，其中许多现在正处于环绕该星球运行的状态。
en: Previously, a machine that can transmit pictures and signals had been sent off to explore the landscape and the chemical makeup of the planet, a number of which are now in the state of circling around the planet.

zh: 迄今为止收集到的证据，已导致许多专家建议尝试进行载人火星任务。
en: The evidence gathered so far, has led to many experts suggesting that a human mission to Mars should be attempted.

zh: 为了使其能够熟练且专业地实现，他们建议各国共同努力提供投资和专业知识。
en: In order for this to happen skilfully and expertly, they have recommended that countries work together to provide investment and expertise.

zh: 需要开展工作以提高航天飞机方向和速度的精确性。
en: Work will need to be undertaken to improve the exactness of the space shuttle's direction and speed.

zh: 在物理学领域工作的人们也建议，需要研究替代燃料和能源。
en: People who work in the area of physics also recommended that alternative fuel and energy sources would need to be researched.

zh: 如果人类能够到达火星，并且关于水隐藏在该行星深处的提议被证明是正确的，那么这将是历史上最伟大的科学相关成就。
en: If humans can reach Mars and the proposed idea that water is hidden deep in the planet proves true, then it would be the greatest science related achievement in history.

zh: 马克·韦特罗斯博士是一个谦逊且特别有才华的人。
en: Dr. Mark Waitrose is a modest and especially talented man.

zh: 在过去的10年里，他一直作为一名研究生物学的人工作。
en: He has been working as a person who studies biology for the past 10 years.

zh: 他随后被任命为一个非常大规模的癌症治疗项目的团队负责人。
en: He was in due course appointed as the team leader of a very large scale cancer treatment programme.

zh: 他与一个专家团队一起，一直在试图找到一种方法来调整DNA中的细胞，以治愈多种癌症。
en: Along with a team of experts, he had been trying to identify a way to adjust cells in DNA to cure a wide range of cancers.

zh: 多亏了五年前的一个“顿悟时刻”，他负责了近年来出现的最激动人心的发展之一。
en: Thanks to an "aha moment" five years ago, he is responsible for one of the most exciting developments to come out in recent years.

zh: “我挣扎了很长时间，然后我决定去度一周假。
en: "I was struggling for so long and then I decided to go on holiday for a week.

zh: 当我坐在海滩上时，它突然出现在我脑海中！”
en: While I was sitting on the beach, it just came to me!"

zh: 他的新想法成功地调整了DNA和基因，以“关闭”某些坏的部分，同时保持健康部分活跃。
en: His new idea succeeded in adapting DNA and genes to "turn off" certain bad ones while keeping the healthy parts active.

zh: 根据韦特罗斯博士的说法，“我们研究的发现非常积极，初步评估表明，许多患有癌症等疾病的患者对治疗反应非常好。
en: According to Dr. Waitrose, "The discoveries of our research have been extremely positive and initial assessments have shown that many patients with illnesses such as cancer, react very well to treatment.

zh: 我们可以看到大多数患者的健康状况有明确的改善，在某些情况下甚至完全康复。
en: We can see definite improvements to the feeling of being well of most patients and in certain cases, even complete recovery.

zh: 作为一个额外的好处，我们发现患有另一种慢性疾病糖尿病的人也可以从该治疗中受益。”
en: As an additional benefit, we find that people who have diabetes, another chronic illness, could also benefit from the treatment."

zh: 我希望有一天能成为著名的发明家或科学家！
en: I would love to be a famous inventor or scientist some day!

zh: 自从我父母给我买了一个显微镜作为生日礼物，我就对科学着迷了！
en: Ever since my parents bought me a microscope for my birthday, I've just been hooked on science!

zh: 我以为你计划加入海军并环游世界呢！
en: I thought you were planning on joining the navy and sailing around the world!

zh: 不过没错，科学现在似乎确实是你主要的娱乐形式了。
en: But yes, science does seem to be your main form of entertainment these days.

zh: 我最近读了很多关于历史上最重要的科学家和发现的资料。
en: I've been reading a lot about the most significant scientists and discoveries in history.

zh: 令人惊奇的是，许多科学家似乎都曾有过一个不可思议的“顿悟时刻”。
en: It's amazing that many scientists seem to have had an incredible "aha moment".

zh: 嗯，这并不总是只关乎一个瞬间。
en: Well, it's not always just about one moment.

zh: 他们可能有了第一个伟大的想法，但他们投入了如此多的辛勤工作。
en: They may have had that first great idea but they put in so much hard work.

zh: 他们花了很多年时间，创造了成千上万的电路和真空管，拨动了成千上万的开关，才实现了他们的目标！
en: It took them many years creating thousands of circuits and tubes and turning on thousands of switches to achieve their goal!

zh: 一个好的想法需要奉献精神才能成功。
en: A good idea needs dedication to be a success.

zh: 你认为最了不起的发明家和科学家还有什么共同点？
en: What else do you think the most amazing inventors and scientists have in common?

zh: 我认为分享你的研究发现极其重要。
en: I think sharing the findings of your research is extremely important.

zh: 斯蒂芬·霍金就是一个很好的例子。
en: A good example is Stephen Hawking.

zh: 他希望每个人都能更多地了解宇宙。
en: He wanted everyone to understand more about the universe.

zh: 即使理论物理是如此复杂！
en: Even if theoretical physics is so complex!

zh: 他理解宇宙的方式，我认为只有爱因斯坦曾经做到过。
en: He perceived the universe in a way that I think only Einstein had before.

zh: 我希望我们能在此基础上继续努力。
en: I hope we can build on his work.

zh: 关于太空、引力、黑洞和时间，还有太多东西需要学习......
en: There's so much more to learn about space, gravity, black holes and time ...

zh: 那么，我们开始吧！
en: Well then, let's get started!

zh: 埃隆·马斯克是一位商人、发明家和工程师，并且无疑是科技界的领军人物之一。
en: Elon Musk is a businessman, inventor and engineer and is undoubtedly, one of the leading figures in the world of technology.

zh: 马斯克1971年出生于南非比勒陀利亚。
en: Musk was born in Pretoria, South Africa in 1971.

zh: 作为一个有天赋的孩子，他在12岁时就自学了计算机编程。
en: A gifted child, he taught himself how to programme computers at the age of 12.

zh: 他被美国的一所大学录取，并在那里完成了文学学士学位。
en: He was accepted to university in the United States where he completed a degree in Arts.

zh: 此后，他在加利福尼亚的斯坦福大学开始攻读物理学博士学位。
en: After this, he started a PhD in physics at Stanford University, California.

zh: 然而，他中途辍学去追求自己在初创企业和科技方面的兴趣。
en: However, he dropped out to pursue his interest in startups and technology.

zh: 他继续在科技领域工作，特别是创办了一个在线支付网站，该网站最终成为了一家全球性公司。
en: He continued working in the field of technology, in particular founding an online payment site that eventually would become a global company.

zh: 2001年，对太空探索感兴趣的马斯克开始与科学家会面，讨论人类在火星上居住的可能性。
en: In 2001 Musk, who had an interest in space exploration, began meeting with scientists to discuss the possibility of human habitation on Mars.

zh: 他相信太空旅行既可以负担得起，也可以为大众所用。
en: He believed that space travel could be both affordable and available for the masses.

zh: 利用自己的财富，马斯克创办了他的
en: Using his own fortune, Musk founded his

zh: 设计火箭和太空飞行器的公司。
en: company that designs rockets and space vehicles.

zh: 这是第一家这样做的私营公司，因为在此之前，几乎所有的太空技术都是由政府设计的。
en: It was the first privately owned company to do so, as prior to this, almost all space technology was designed by governments.

zh: 2012年，该公司制造的火箭与国际空间站对接。
en: In 2012, the rockets made by the company docked with the International Space Station.

zh: 马斯克还从NASA和美国航天局获得了合同，从而证明了商业太空探索的概念既可行又负担得起，创造了历史。
en: Musk also received contracts from NASA and the US space agency, thus making history by proving that the concept of commercial space exploration was both possible and affordable.

zh: 马斯克的另一项创新是电动汽车。
en: Another one of Musk's innovations is the electric car.

zh: 为了制造电动汽车，马斯克创办了一家公司，并于2008年成为首席执行官。
en: To create electric cars, Musk started a company and became the CEO in 2008.

zh: 该公司以塞尔维亚裔美国发明家和物理学家尼古拉·特斯拉的名字命名，他最著名的成就是设计了交流电系统。
en: The company is named after Nicolai Tesla, the Serbian-American inventor and physicist, who is best known for designing the AC electrical system.

zh: 马斯克公司制造的汽车旨在结束对化石燃料的依赖，从而减少气候变化和汽车空气污染的负面影响。
en: The cars made by Musk's company are designed to end the dependence on fossil fuels and so reduce the negative effects of climate change and air pollution from cars.

zh: 马斯克的创新动机是扩大人类在宇宙中的触及范围并保护我们星球的资源。
en: Musks motivation for his innovations are to expand human reach in the universe and protect the resources of our planet.

zh: 通过创造探索新行星的机会，马斯克相信，如果地球发生大灾难，人类有可能在其他地方继续生存。
en: By creating opportunities to explore new planets, Musk believes that in the event of a major catastrophe on Earth, there would be the potential for the human race to continue elsewhere.

zh: 在地球上，马斯克的发明旨在寻找可再生的、环境友好的解决方案，以应对日益增长的全球人口对能源和交通的需求挑战。
en: On Earth, Musk's inventions are designed to find renewable, environment-friendly solutions to meet the challenge of an ever-growing global population that demands energy and transport.

zh: 马斯克说：“我得出的结论是，我们应该渴望扩大人类意识的范围和规模，以便更好地理解该问什么问题。
en: According to Musk, "I came to the conclusion that we should aspire to increase the scope and scale of human consciousness in order to better understand what questions to ask.

zh: 真的，唯一有意义的事情就是为更大的集体启蒙而奋斗。”
en: Really, the only thing that makes sense is to strive for greater collective enlightenment."

zh: 在肯定句中，may, could, might 用于表示某事可能发生或可能存在，但 can 不能用于此目的。
en: In affirmative sentences, may, could, might can be used to say there is a possibility of something happening or being true, but not can.

zh: might 表示可能性很小。
en: Might can suggest that there is less possibility.

zh: 今天晚些时候可能会下雪。
en: It may/might/could snow later in the day.

zh: 根据天气预报，今天可能会下雨。
en: According to the weather report, it may rain today.

zh: can 用来表示更宽泛的某事发生的可能性。
en: Can is used to talk about a more general possibility of something happening.

zh: 过多的雪会带来麻烦。
en: Too much snow can cause trouble.

zh: 在否定句中，用 cannot 或者 couldn't 表示“不可能”。
en: In negative sentences, we use cannot or couldn't to say that something is not the case.

zh: 他说他不会再和我说话了。
en: He said he wouldn't talk to me any more.

zh: can 用于问句。
en: Can is used in questions.

zh: 有人敲门。
en: There is a knock at the door.

zh: 这么晚了会是谁呢？
en: Who can it be at this time of the night?

zh: 在肯定句中 may / could / might + have done 用于表示过去某事可能发生了。
en: In affirmative sentences, may / could / might + have done is used to say it is possible that something happened in the past.

zh: 我以为珍妮生我的气了，但我可能/也许/说不定是错了。
en: I thought Jenny was angry at me, but I may / might / could have been wrong.

zh: 没人知道汤姆在哪里。
en: Nobody knew where Tom was.

zh: 他可能/也许/说不定出国了。
en: He may / might / could have gone abroad.

zh: might / could + have done 用于表示某事可能在过去发生，但事实上并没有发生。
en: We can use might / could+ have done to say that something was possible in the past, but we know that it did not in fact happen.

zh: may 和 can 不能用于此种情况。
en: In this case we can't use may or can.

zh: 如果你今天早上步行去学校，你本可以/本可能避免那场事故。
en: If you went to school on foot this morning, you could / might have avoided the accident.

zh: 形容词修饰名词或代词，提供更多的信息。
en: An adjective modifies (gives us more information about) a noun or a pronoun.

zh: 形容词有两类:
en: There are two classes of adjectives:

zh: 性质形容词: 例如 blue, cheerful, short, hot...
en: qualitative: e.g. blue, cheerful, short, hot...

zh: 类别形容词: 例如 Chinese, Italian, oval, female ...
en: classifying: e.g. Chinese, Italian, oval, female ...

zh: 性质形容词总是置于类别形容词前。
en: Qualitative adjectives always come before classifying adjectives.

zh: 用以下方法确定某个形容词是性质形容词还是类别形容词:
en: Use the following methods to decide if an adjective is qualitative or classifying:

zh: 性质形容词有比较级和最高级，如 hotter, hottest，而类别形容词没有这种变化。
en: You can use qualitative adjectives (but not classifying adjectives) in the comparative and superlative form, e.g. hotter, hottest.

zh: 性质形容词可以用 extremely, very 等词修饰，而类别形容词不能用这些词修饰。
en: Qualitative adjectives can also be modified by a word such as extremely, very, etc while classifying adjectives cannot.

zh: 形容词的一般排序规则:
en: The most commom order of adjectives:

zh: The attractive young girl wore a beautiful blue dress.
en: The attractive young girl wore a beautiful blue dress.

zh: The tiny silver balls looked attractive on the birthday cake.
en: The tiny silver balls looked attractive on the birthday cake.

zh: The short elderly lady walked proudly into the room.
en: The short elderly lady walked proudly into the room.

zh: The handsome Italian man was wearing expensive clothes.
en: The handsome Italian man was wearing expensive clothes.

zh: We both came to completely different conclusions.
en: We both came to completely different conclusions.

zh: That terribly depressed clown was named Grock.
en: That terribly depressed clown was named Grock.

zh: The painting was incredibly beautiful.
en: The painting was incredibly beautiful.

zh: The truly happy bride and groom went on honeymoon.
en: The truly happy bride and groom went on honeymoon.

zh: The twins had very different characteristics.
en: The twins had very different characteristics.

zh: 合成形容词是由两个或多个形容词或名词通过连字符连接构成的新词。
en: Compound adjectives are formed when two or more adjectives are joined together by a hyphen to create a new word.

zh: 我最后拍照的人是我妹妹黛西，那是上周末我们在公园闲逛的时候。
en: The last person I took a photo of was my sister Daisy when we were hanging out last weekend at the park.

zh: 如有必要，我们总是求同存异。
en: We always agree to disagree if we need to.

zh: 我们现在已经解决了我们的问题。
en: We've sorted out our problems now.

zh: 兄弟之间的差异不能割断他们的血缘关系。
en: Differences between brothers cannot sever their blood ties.

zh: ......通过不超过另外五个人的联结。
en: ... through a chain of no more than five other people.

zh: 一旦包裹被此人接收，他或她就会将包裹发送给他们的一个联系人，直到包裹可以亲自送达正确的人手中。
en: Once the parcel had been received by this person, he or she would send the parcel onto a contact of theirs until the parcel could be personally delivered to the correct person.

zh: 我们知道你参与了那里的一个研究项目。
en: We know that you are involved in a research project there.

zh: 在罗塞托镇，几乎所有的家庭都是三代同堂。
en: In Roseto, almost all households consisted of three generations.

zh: 20世纪60年代，年青一代的罗塞托人不愿意保持传统的生活方式。
en: In the 1960s, younger Rosetans would not keep up the traditional lifestyle.

zh: 突然，这组人笑了起来。
en: Suddenly, the group burst into laughter.

zh: 这个国家正在陷入最严重的经济危机。
en: The country was falling into the depths of economic crisis.

zh: 玛丽拉收拾完盘子，紧紧地皱着眉头，也回到房间休息了。
en: And to bed, when she had put her dishes away, went Marilla, frowning most resolutely.

zh: 吸引儿童的表演特别受欢迎。
en: The acts appealing to children were particularly popular.

zh: 该故事长期受到各类人士的欢迎。
en: The story has long appealed to all tastes.

zh: 设计必须要雅俗共赏，老幼皆宜。
en: The design has to appeal to all ages and social groups!

zh: 我为我们的行为道歉。
en: I apologise for our part in it.

zh: 他们没有勇气为自己的行为道歉。
en: They do not have the courage to apologise for their actions.

zh: 天下至柔驰骋天下至坚。
en: The softest things in the world overcome the hardest things in the world.

zh: 摇滚乐队“暴风雨”的鼓手马明不得不收拾行李。
en: Ma Ming, drummer for the rock band "Storm", had to pack his bags.

zh: 我必须在晚饭前完成作业。
en: I have to finish my homework before dinner.

zh: 房东说如果他早知道马明是个鼓手，就不会把房子租给他。
en: The flat-owner said if he had known that Ma Ming was a drummer, he wouldn't have rented the flat to him.

zh: 一位委员会成员说：我们告诉他应该停止打鼓，否则就搬走。
en: We told him that he ought to cease drumming or leave the property, a council member said.

zh: 住在旅馆里就意味着每天有服务员整理床铺，我也不必自己洗衣服！
en: Living in a hotel means the hotel staff makes the bed every day and I don't have to do my own washing!

zh: 我们很难去掉（身上的）鱼腥味！
en: We could hardly get rid of the smell of fish!

zh: 然而，史密斯从阳台上倒掉他的一个大鱼缸中的水，将迈奇夫妇和蛋糕都淋了个通透。
en: Instead, Smith emptied one of his larger tanks over his balcony and both the McKays and the cake were wet through.

zh: 他还承诺从现在开始改变他的方式。
en: He has also promised to change his ways from now on.

zh: 他们只是希望如果坚持的时间足够长，情况能够慢慢地好起来。
en: They just hope that if they hold out for long enough, things will get better by and by.

zh: 然后，你提出解决方案。
en: Then, you put forward solutions.

zh: 杜沙的腿受了伤，几乎不省人事，但他被一堆尸体覆盖，幸存下来。
en: Chuc was wounded in the leg and almost unconscious, but he was covered by a pile of dead bodies and thus, his life was saved.

zh: 他们中的一个人用英语说，他在英国工作过几年，受够了这场战争，如果战争结束，他会很高兴。
en: One of their men, speaking in English, said that he had worked in England for some years and that he was fed up to the neck with this war and would be glad when it was over.

zh: 我们建立了一种默契，整个节礼日，我们都没有开过一枪，他们也一样; 双方似乎都在等对方先开火。
en: We'd formed a bond and during the whole of Boxing Day, we never fired a shot and they the same; each side seemed to be waiting for the other to set the ball rolling.

zh: 这是这个病人第二次来到我们这里，而这次他的双腿都被炸没了——他也就才二十几岁。
en: It was this patient's second visit to us and this time he'd had both his legs blown off — he was all-of-about 20 years old.

zh: 很多国家已经立法禁止烟草产品广告，如香烟。
en: In many countries, there are already laws which do not allow advertising for tobacco products like cigarettes.

zh: 如果孩子在电视上看不到做这些事的广告，他们很可能就不会去模仿他们的行为。
en: If children see fewer advertisements on TV of people doing these activities, they are much less likely to try to copy their behaviour.

zh: 投资在不健康产品上的钱可以拿来做更值得的事，例如改善环境。
en: The money invested in making these unhealthy products could be used for more worthwhile businesses, such as those that improve our environment.

zh: 医学上的进步范围很广，从利用显微镜揭示病因，到施行手术，用捐赠的器官做器官移植。
en: Medical advances ranged from discovering the causes of diseases under microscopes to staging operations to replace diseased organs with donated ones.

zh: 我们开始坐飞机周游世界，把人造卫星发射至轨道。同时，科学家们发现了如何分裂原子。在此之前，原子被认为是宇宙中最小的物质微粒。
en: We started flying around the world, launching satellites into orbit and, at the same time, scientists figured out how to split the atom, previously thought to be the smallest particle of matter in the universe.

zh: 尽管不太可能选出哪项发现是最重要的，但是挑选出20世纪的几位先驱还是可能的。
en: Although it is impossible to choose the most important discovery, it is possible to single out a few pioneers of the 20th century.

zh: 它有 18,000 个显像管，数千条电路和 6,000 个开关，耗电量异常巨大，以致于当它启动的时候，当地所有的灯都熄灭了!
en: With 18,000 tubes, thousands of circuits and 6,000 switches, it used so much energy that when it was turned on, the lights in the local town went out!

zh: 这些 20 世纪的先驱都致力于改善地球上人类的生活质量。
en: These pioneers of the 20th century were all dedicated to improving the quality of human life on Earth.

zh: 尽管霍金患有疾病，但他并不认为自己是个不幸的人。
en: In spite of his disease, Hawking didn't consider himself an unlucky man.

zh: 他说，这并没有阻碍他拥有家庭，也没有阻碍他在工作中取得成功。
en: He said it had not prevented him from having a family, and being successful in his work.

zh: 他进一步思考了这个过程，评估了加大水量将产生多少能量。
en: He reflected further on the process, estimating how much more power could be created by a larger amount of water.

zh: 后来他开始用蒸汽做实验，最后改进了蒸汽机。
en: Later he began to experiment with steam, and eventually developed the steam engine.

zh: 拿出你的手机，看看你的照片。
en: Take your mobile phone and look at your photos.

zh: 你最后一张照片拍的是谁？
en: Who was the last person you took a photo of?

zh: 我最后一张照片拍的是我妹妹黛西，上周末我们在公园里玩的时候拍的。
en: The last person I took a photo of was my sister Daisy when we were hanging out last weekend at the park.

zh: 那你和你妹妹相处得好吗？
en: Do you get on well with your sister then?

zh: 是的。我们彼此相处得非常融洽。
en: Yes. We get on very well with each other.

zh: 我们年龄很接近，所以小时候经常一起玩。
en: We're very close in age so we used to play with each other when we were children.

zh: 我们有一些共同的朋友。
en: We have some of the same friends.

zh: 你们小时候难道不吵架或打架吗？
en: Didn't you argue or fight with each other when you were children?

zh: 嗯，有时会有冲突，但从来不会持续很久。
en: Well, sometimes conflicts will come up, but never for very long.

zh: 如果有必要，我们总是同意各自保留意见。
en: We always agree to disagree if we need to.

zh: 最重要的是，每当有人需要帮助或只是一点鼓励时，我们总是在彼此身边。
en: The most important thing is that we are always there for each other whenever one needs some help or just a bit of encouragement.

zh: 马克斯，你和304公寓的邻居还有问题吗？
en: Max, are you still having problems with your neighbours in Flat 304?

zh: 我们现在已经解决了我们的问题。
en: We've sorted out our problems now.

zh: 实际上，你和他们之间是什么问题？
en: What was the problem you were having with them, actually?

zh: 基本上，他们晚上在公寓里制造了太多噪音，这真的打扰了我们。
en: Basically, they were making too much noise in their flat in the evenings and it was really disturbing us.

zh: 我们根本不想和他们有任何冲突。
en: We didn't want any conflict with them at all.

zh: 我们希望与邻居保持友好关系，所以我们都有良好的社区精神。
en: We want to have friendly relationships with our neighbours, so we all have a good community spirit.

zh: 那么，你最后是怎么处理噪音问题的？
en: So, how did you deal with the noise problem in the end?

zh: 我们没有待在自己的公寓里，对噪音感到愤怒，而是去了他们的公寓，告诉他们噪音让我们多么不开心，他们道了歉，并说从那时起会减少噪音。
en: Instead of staying in our flat, feeling angry about the noise, we went over to their flat, told them how unhappy the noise was making us and they apologised and said they would make less noise from then on.

zh: 现在我们相处得非常好。
en: Now we get on really well with each other.

zh: 早上好，史密斯博士。欢迎来到我们的节目。
en: Good morning, Dr. Smith. Welcome to our programme.

zh: 我们注意到您多次访问一个叫罗塞托的小镇。
en: We noticed that you have paid quite a few visits to a small town called Roseto.

zh: 我们知道您在那里参与了一个研究项目。
en: We know that you are involved in a research project there.

zh: 您能告诉我们更多关于它的情况吗？
en: Would you please tell us more about it?

zh: 实际上，我的研究是基于一项早期关于罗塞托生活方式的研究。
en: Actually, my research was based on an earlier study of the lifestyle in Roseto.

zh: 在20世纪50年代，心脏病在美国是一种流行病，但医生发现几乎没有65岁以下的罗塞托人经历过心脏病。
en: In the 1950s, heart attacks were an epidemic in the USA, but doctors found there was hardly a Rosetan below 65 who'd experienced one.

zh: 人们死于年老，而不是疾病。
en: People were dying of old age rather than from illness.

zh: 此外，犯罪率非常低。
en: Additionally, there was very little crime.

zh: 这是一个非常安全的小镇。
en: This was a very safe small town.

zh: 根据一项关于罗塞托的早期研究，研究人员发现罗塞托人心脏病低发率的原因在于社区本身。
en: According to an earlier study about Roseto, researchers found that the reason for the low rate of Rosetans' heart attacks was the community itself.

zh: 罗塞托是一个非常紧密的社区。
en: Roseto was a very closely-knit community.

zh: 那里的人们互相照顾。
en: People there looked after each other.

zh: 他们互相拜访，并经常组织社区庆祝活动。
en: They visited each other and frequently organised community celebrations.

zh: 在罗塞托，几乎所有的家庭都由三代人组成。
en: In Roseto, almost all households consisted of three generations.

zh: 他们所享有的社交网络保护他们免受现代世界的压力。
en: The social network they enjoyed protected them from the pressure of the modern world.

zh: 那么后来几年的情况如何？
en: Then what was the situation in later years?

zh: 在20世纪60年代，年轻的罗塞托人不再维持传统的生活方式。
en: In the 1960s, younger Rosetans would not keep up the traditional lifestyle.

zh: 他们搬到了城镇郊区的典型独栋房屋。
en: They moved to typical single-family houses on the outskirts of the town.

zh: 他们摒弃了传统的社会纽带，
en: They rejected traditional social bonds,

zh: 从那以后发生了什么？
en: What has happened since then?

zh: 嗯，变化的后果很快就显现了。
en: Well, the consequences of the change followed quickly.

zh: 1971年，发生了第一例45岁以下的罗塞托人心脏病发作。
en: The first heart attack of a Rosetan under 45 happened in 1971.

zh: 如今，罗塞托的心脏病发病率与平均水平相同。
en: Today the rate of heart attacks in Roseto is the same as the average.

zh: 妈妈总是告诉我她的家乡很小，但这就像一个大城市。
en: Mum always tells me her hometown is small, but this is like a big city.

zh: 嗯，你妈妈在这里的时候不是这样的。
en: Well, it wasn't like this when your mum was here.

zh: 那时我们的镇子很小。
en: Our town was small then.

zh: 没有很多高楼大厦。
en: There weren't many tall buildings.

zh: 那时候，我们不住在公寓里。
en: Back then, we didn't live in flats.

zh: 我们住在一所有两间小房间的房子里，不像今天的大房子。
en: We lived in a sort of house, with two small rooms, not like the big houses today.

zh: 厨房和厕所在房子外面。
en: The kitchen and toilet were outside the house.

zh: 我们和邻居共用它们。
en: We shared them with our neighbours.

zh: 那不方便，是吗？
en: That was not convenient, was it?

zh: 是有点不方便，但当时我们并没有感觉到。
en: It was a bit inconvenient, but we didn't feel that at that time.

zh: 实际上，我们和邻居关系很亲密。
en: Actually, we were close to our neighbours.

zh: 我们彼此友好，就像一个大家庭。
en: We were friendly to each other, just like a kind of big family.

zh: 生活在一个紧密的社区里很棒。
en: It's great to live in a closely-knit community.

zh: 孩子们放学后一起玩耍，邻居们也经常互相拜访。
en: Children played together after school, and neighbours often visited each other.

zh: 你知道，有些老邻居现在仍然是我最好的朋友，尽管我们现在住得很远。
en: You know, some old neighbours are still my best friends, although we live far away now.

zh: 你是说今天下午要来的那些朋友吗？他们都是你的老邻居吗？
en: You mean the friends who are coming this afternoon? Are they all your old neighbours?

zh: 是的。你可以请他们给你讲讲你妈妈的童年故事。
en: Yes. You can ask them to tell you your mum's childhood stories.

zh: 下午好，我能为您做些什么？
en: Good afternoon, how may I help you?

zh: 我当然希望有人能！你们那个会计部门根本不知道他们在做什么！
en: I certainly hope somebody can! That accounts department of yours doesn't know what they are doing!

zh: 请您告诉我出了什么问题好吗？
en: Would you please tell me what is wrong?

zh: 这是我第三次为同一件事打电话了。这简直是在浪费我的时间。
en: This is the third time I am calling about the same thing. It's just a waste of my time.

zh: 是的，先生，我完全理解您为什么心烦。请告诉我问题是什么。
en: Yes, sir, I do understand why you are upset. Tell me what the problem is.

zh: 是我的账户问题。我已经全额付款了，但你们一直发消息说我需要立即付款，否则我的账户将被关闭。
en: It's my account. I have paid it in full but you keep sending messages to say that I need to pay immediately, otherwise my account will be closed.

zh: 先生，请告诉我您的账号，我会查询并确保问题尽快得到解决。
en: Please give me your account number, sir, and I'll inquire and make sure the issue is resolved as soon as possible.

zh: 我叫琼斯，我的账号是J28760。
en: The name's Jones and my account number is J28760.

zh: 好的。请您稍等几分钟好吗？我会检查一下问题，看看我能做些什么。
en: Right. Would you please wait a few minutes? I will check the problem and see what I can do.

zh: 抱歉让您久等了。我们检测到您的账户有一个错误。我已经重置了您的账户。一小时内就会恢复正常。一切都会好起来的。
en: Sorry for keeping you waiting. We detected an error with your account. I have reset your account. It'll be okay in an hour. Everything will be fine.

zh: 非常感谢。很抱歉我刚才那么生气。我知道这不是你的错。
en: Thank you very much. I'm sorry I was so angry. I know it is not your fault.

zh: 先生，我非常理解您的感受。我为我们在这件事中的过错道歉。
en: I quite understand how you feel, sir. I apologise for our part in it.

zh: 谢谢你。你帮了大忙了。
en: Thank you. You have been most helpful.

zh: 这是我的荣幸，先生。祝您有愉快的一天。
en: It's a pleasure, sir. Have a good day.

zh: 你好，史密斯先生。我最近有点生自己的气。我想我需要一些帮助。
en: Hello, Mr Smith. I'm somewhat annoyed with myself recently. I think I need some help.

zh: 嗨，蒂姆！过来坐。我想听听你的问题，看看我能做些什么。
en: Hi, Tim! Come and sit down. I'd like to listen to your problem and see what I can do.

zh: 我很惭愧地承认，我在玩手机游戏方面真的很难控制自己。
en: I'm ashamed to admit that I really struggle with myself about playing mobile games.

zh: 当我坐下来学习时，我常常打算先玩几分钟手机游戏，而一旦我这么做了，我就停不下来。
en: When I sit down to study, I often intend to start by playing mobile games for a few minutes and once I do so, I cannot stop.

zh: 过了一会儿，我注意到时间过得很快，这让我感到内疚。
en: After a while, I notice the time passing quickly and this makes me feel guilty.

zh: 但第二天，一切又
en: But the next day, everything goes

zh: 这在青少年中是完全正常的，甚至在成年人中也是如此，这是完全可以理解的。
en: That's completely normal among teenagers, even among adults, and it's perfectly understandable.

zh: 有勇气承认这一点已经是成熟的表现。
en: To have the courage to acknowledge this is already a sign of maturity.

zh: 谢谢你，但我能做些什么来阻止它呢？
en: Thank you, but what can I do to stop it?

zh: 首先，我想推荐在图书馆学习。
en: Firstly, I would like to recommend studying in a library.

zh: 这是因为图书馆通常有良好的学习氛围，这将帮助你专注于你的工作。
en: This is because the library usually has good studying atmosphere, which will help you concentrate on your work.

zh: 那真是个不错的主意。
en: That's a really good idea.

zh: 看到其他学生努力学习也会鼓励我努力工作。
en: Seeing other students working hard will encourage me to work hard, too.

zh: 但我不会总是在图书馆学习，所以当我在家时我该怎么办？
en: But I won't always be able to study in the library, so what should I do when I'm at home?

zh: 当你在家时，你可以请你的妈妈或爸爸帮忙监督你，确保你每天放学后学习两个小时。
en: When you're at home, you can ask your mum or dad to help monitor you and make sure that you are working for two hours after school every day.

zh: 我相信你的父母会很高兴帮助你。
en: And I'm sure your parents would be happy to help you.

zh: 但是当他们不在家的时候我该怎么办呢？
en: But what should I do when they aren't at home though?

zh: 这就是你需要采取自我控制和目标设定的地方。
en: This is where you need to adopt self-control and goal setting.

zh: 为每个学习阶段设定一个明确的目标。
en: Set a clear goal for every studying period.

zh: 通过给自己设定一个目标，你会知道在时间结束前你必须完成什么。
en: By assigning yourself a goal, you will know what you have to finish by the end of the time.

zh: 当你想拿起手机时，不断告诉自己再坚持十分钟，直到你最终达到目标。
en: And when you want to pick up your phone, keep telling yourself to hold on for another ten minutes until finally you reach your goal.

zh: 我认为你的建议很有帮助。
en: I think your advice is helpful.

zh: 今天我们要谈论冲突，以及如何处理它。
en: Today we're going to talk about conflict, and how to deal with it.

zh: 今天我们请来了城市大学的马圭尔博士。
en: We have Dr. Maguire, from City University with us today.

zh: 马圭尔博士，在我们的日常生活中，我们可能会遇到各种各样的冲突。
en: Dr. Maguire, in our daily life, we may come across various conflicts.

zh: 例如，邻居抱怨音乐声极大直到深夜，父母因为孩子花太多时间上网而对他们非常生气。
en: For example, neighbours complain of extremely loud music until very late at night and parents become really angry with their children for spending too much time online.

zh: 在你看来，处理冲突的最佳方式是什么？
en: In your opinion, what is the best way to deal with conflict?

zh: 嗯，有三种常见的方法。
en: Well, there are three common ways.

zh: 第一种方法是用更多的愤怒来回应愤怒。
en: The first way is to respond to anger with more anger.

zh: 这意味着你对冲突情况的反应更加消极和暴力。
en: This means you react more negatively and violently to a situation of conflict.

zh: 这是处理冲突最糟糕的方式，因为情况可能会变得更糟，甚至导致暴力。
en: This is the worst way of handling conflict, because the situation can get worse or even lead to violence.

zh: 不幸的是，这是对冲突最常见的反应方式之一。
en: Unfortunately, it is one of the most common ways of reacting to conflict.

zh: 另一种效果不太好的方式是什么都不做。
en: Another way, which doesn't work very well, is doing nothing.

zh: 这是指一个人什么也不说，试图逃避或忽视这种情况。
en: This is when the person says nothing and tries to escape or ignore the situation.

zh: 他们只是希望如果他们坚持足够长的时间，事情会逐渐好转。
en: They just hope that if they hold out for long enough, things will get better by and by.

zh: 这并不能解决任何问题，而且退缩的人会对另一个人感到越来越愤怒。
en: This does not solve any problem and the person who withdraws feels more and more angry with the other person.

zh: 把情绪压抑在心里可能是非常不健康的。
en: Holding your feelings inside can be very unhealthy.

zh: 另一种可能性是向局外人寻求帮助。
en: Another possibility is to ask for help from somebody outside the situation.

zh: 你到底是什么意思？
en: What do you mean exactly?

zh: 嗯，这就是当你请一个局外人来，由他们决定应该怎么做。
en: Well, this is when you call on somebody outside the situation, and they decide what should happen.

zh: 这可能很有用，特别是当冲突很严重的时候。
en: This can be useful, especially if the conflict is serious.

zh: 然而，局外人可能不了解全部情况。
en: However, people outside the situation may not know the whole story.

zh: 他们通常无法做出正确的判断。
en: They are usually not in the position to make the right judgment.

zh: 毫无疑问，最好的办法是尝试与对方谈判。
en: Without doubt, the best thing to do is to try to negotiate with the other person.

zh: 好的。非常感谢您，马奎尔博士。
en: Right. Thank you very much, Dr. Maguire.

zh: 我们休息一下，稍后回来。
en: Let's have a break and we'll be back soon.

zh: 感谢您与我们在一起。
en: Thank you for staying with us.

zh: 刚才，马奎尔博士建议了几件我们在处理冲突时不应该做的事情。
en: Just now, Dr. Maguire suggested several things we should not do when dealing with conflict.

zh: 那么我们应该怎么做呢，马奎尔博士？
en: So what should we do then, Dr. Maguire?

zh: 要进行谈判，首先，你不应该表现得好像你100%正确而他们100%错误。
en: To negotiate, first, you should not act as if you're 100% right and they are 100% wrong.

zh: 接下来，你应该尝试清楚地解释你想要什么。
en: Next, you should try to explain clearly what you want.

zh: 如果对方愤怒地回应，你不应该以同样的方式回应。
en: If the other person replies angrily, you shouldn't respond in the same way.

zh: 最好的办法是用不同的方式重复你所说的话，然后试着找出对方想要什么，以及为什么。
en: The best thing is to repeat what you said in a different way, and then try to find out what the other person wants, and why.

zh: 当我发现对方想要什么时，我该怎么办？
en: When I find out what the other person wants, what should I do?

zh: 然后，你提出解决方案。
en: Then, you put forward solutions.

zh: 确保每个人都同意是非常重要的。
en: It's very important to make sure that everybody agrees.

zh: 如果你同意做某事，那么就要确保它实现。
en: And if you agree to do something, then make sure it comes about.

zh: 好的。我们还有什么应该做的吗？
en: OK. Is there anything else we ought to do?

zh: 嗯，你应该清晰而坚定地说话，但绝不应该大喊大叫。
en: Well, you should speak clearly and firmly, but you should never shout.

zh: 另外，注意你的肢体语言。
en: Also, be careful of your body language.

zh: 试着放松，并且最好不要站得离对方太近。
en: Try to relax, and it's always better never to stand too close to the other person.

zh: 谢谢您，马奎尔博士，感谢您非常有用的建议。
en: Thank you, Dr. Maguire, for your very useful advice.

zh: 今天我从年度青年发明家颁奖现场进行报道。
en: Today I'm reporting from the Young Inventors of the Year Awards.

zh: 其中一位年轻的发明家，苏珊·李，现在就在我身边。
en: One of the young inventors, Susan Li, is here with me now.

zh: 苏珊，你认为发明家具有哪些特质？
en: Susan, what characteristics do you think inventors have?

zh: 嗯，我认为发明家对理解世界以及如何改善我们的生活很感兴趣。
en: Well, I think inventors are interested in understanding the world and how our lives can be improved.

zh: 他们富有创造力和好奇心。
en: They are creative and curious.

zh: 我还想说发明家具有跳出框架思维的能力。
en: I would also say inventors think outside the box.

zh: 他们以不同的方式看待世界。
en: They see the world differently.

zh: 那么你能告诉我们一些关于你的发明吗？
en: So would you please tell us something about your invention?

zh: 我的发明是一种可以放入海洋的塑料和垃圾收集装置。
en: My invention is a type of plastic and garbage gathering device that can be placed into the ocean.

zh: 它可以帮助解决水污染问题。
en: It can help solve water pollution.

zh: 它最大的优点是......
en: The biggest advantage is that it is...

zh: 听起来太不可思议了！你们还有什么正在努力解决的问题吗？
en: I: That sounds incredible! Are there any problems you are still trying to solve?

zh: 是的，目前生产成本还很高。
en: S: Yes, it is expensive to produce at the moment.

zh: 我希望它能很快投入使用。
en: I: I hope it will be put into operation soon.

zh: 你是一位伟大的发明家！感谢您接受我们的采访！
en: You are a great inventor! Thank you for speaking to us!

zh: 好的，陈曦、李珍，让我们开始这个项目吧！
en: CY: OK, Chen Xi and Li Zhen, let's get this project started!

zh: 丁老师让我们思考一下我们日常生活中最有用的发明。
en: Ms Ding asked us to think of the most useful invention in our everyday life.

zh: 你们俩觉得是什么？
en: What do you both think?

zh: 对。嗯，对我来说，那一定是电脑。
en: CX: Right. Well, for me, it would have to be computers.

zh: 我花很多时间在电脑上学习、玩游戏、和朋友聊天......
en: I spend so much time on my computer studying, playing games, chatting with friends ...

zh: 电脑是个不错的选择，但真正让它们变得有用的是互联网的发明。
en: L: Computers are a good choice, but it really was the invention of the Internet that made them useful.

zh: 在那之前，只有科学家和其他技术人员使用它们。
en: Before that, only scientists and other technical people used them.

zh: 现在，因为有了互联网，我们每个人都有理由拥有一台电脑了。
en: Now, because of the Internet, there is a reason for us all to have one.

zh: 互联网是一项了不起的发明。
en: The Internet is an amazing invention.

zh: 在那之前，人们只能通过电视、报纸和书籍获取信息。
en: Before that, people had only TV, newspapers and books to get information.

zh: 当然，在互联网出现之前，你不能在网上和朋友聊天，也不能收发电子邮件。
en: CX: And of course before the Internet, you couldn't chat with friends online or send and receive emails.

zh: 只有电话和信件。
en: Only telephone calls and letters.

zh: 哇，那么互联网是什么时候发明的？
en: L: Wow, so when was the Internet invented?

zh: 嗯。好的......它发明于20世纪60年代，但直到20世纪90年代才被广泛使用。
en: CY: Hmmmm. OK... It was invented in the 1960s, but it wasn't widely used until the 1990s.

zh: 在如此短的时间内，生活发生了多么大的变化啊！
en: CX: How life has been transformed in such a short time!

zh: 想象一下未来50年会发生什么！
en: Imagine what's coming in the next 50 years!

zh: 你是否曾有过这样的经历：你连续几天都在做一些创造性的工作，但进展甚微；突然一个想法飞入你的脑海？
en: Have you ever had such an experience: you are working on some creative jobs for several days but little progress was made; suddenly an idea flies into your mind?

zh: 然后你忍不住大喊：“啊哈！就是这样！”
en: Then you can't help shouting out: "Aha! That's it!"

zh: 这种灵光一现非常奇妙，不是吗？
en: Such flash of insight is quite wonderful, isn't it?

zh: 灵感是如何产生的？
en: How does the inspiration come?

zh: 为什么有些人工作如此努力却收效甚微，而另一些人似乎什么都没做却有好点子冒出来？
en: Why do some people work so hard but achieve very little, while some others seem to do nothing but good ideas emerge?

zh: 今天，我们邀请邦德博士来谈谈创造过程。
en: Today, we invite Dr. Bond to talk about the creative process.

zh: 每个人都可能经历过某种“顿悟时刻”。
en: Everyone may have experienced some kinds of "aha moments".

zh: 但对此有很多误解。
en: But there's much misunderstanding about it.

zh: 有些人认为这是巧合发生的。
en: Some think it happens by coincidence.

zh: 事实上，它实际上来自于你一直在付出的努力。
en: In fact, it actually comes from the efforts you have been constantly making.

zh: 你的意思是，突然的想法来自于我们之前一直在做的努力？
en: H: Do you mean that sudden ideas come from the efforts we have been making before?

zh: 在一个绝妙的想法背后，往往包含着难以置信的辛勤工作。
en: Behind a brilliant idea, there's often incredible hard work involved in it.

zh: 例如，一位科学家的巨大成功并非一蹴而就。
en: For example, great success of a scientist did not come all at once.

zh: 相反，他/她必须投入多年的研究才能有所发现。
en: Instead, he / she has to invest many years of study before making a discovery.

zh: 伟大的科学家阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦在摇他的宝宝时，似乎受到了启发，从而发现了“相对论”。
en: The great scientist Albert Einstein seemed to be inspired in discovering the "theory of relativity" when he was rocking his baby.

zh: 但实际上，他多年来一直潜心研究它。
en: But actually, he had buried himself a great deal in the study of it for years.

zh: 但这是否意味着努力工作会导致“顿悟时刻”呢？
en: But does it mean hard work will lead to an "aha moment"?

zh: 创造过程有四个阶段。
en: There are four stages in the creative process.

zh: 第一阶段是准备。
en: The first stage is preparation.

zh: 接下来的三个阶段是酝酿、评估和阐述。
en: And the next three stages are incubation, evaluation and elaboration.

zh: 我将在第二节课中进一步解释它们。
en: I will explain them further in the second period.

zh: 好的，非常感谢您，邦德博士。
en: OK, thank you very much, Dr. Bond.

zh: 那么邦德博士，您刚才谈到了“顿悟时刻”可能发生之前的四个阶段。
en: So Dr. Bond, just now you talked about the four stages before an "aha moment" may occur.

zh: 您能告诉我们更多关于这些阶段的信息吗？
en: Would you please tell us more about the stages?

zh: 正如我所说，第一阶段是“准备”。
en: As I said, the first stage is "preparation".

zh: 你需要深入参与到这个领域或区域中。
en: You need to become deeply involved in the field or area.

zh: 您能解释一下“酝酿”是什么意思吗？
en: Could you explain what "incubation" means?

zh: 在你深入参与一个领域之后，你什么也不做。
en: After you deeply become involved in an area, you do nothing.

zh: 你只是沉浸其中。
en: You just bury yourself in it.

zh: 例如，莫扎特过去常常每天花几个小时，只是四处走走。
en: For example, Mozart used to spend hours a day, just walking around.

zh: 然后突然之间，他就有了“顿悟体验”。
en: Then suddenly, he had the "aha experience".

zh: 这是否意味着“什么都不做”？
en: Does that mean "doing nothing"?

zh: 嗯，我们大多数人认为，如果我们不做任何有意义或有创意的事情，那就是在浪费时间。
en: Well, most of us think it's a waste of time if we do nothing meaningful or creative.

zh: 事实上，情况往往并非如此。
en: In fact, this is not often the case.

zh: 我们都需要明白的是，创造性的见解需要时间来成长或出现。
en: All we need to understand is that creative insights take time to grow or emerge.

zh: 我们不应该担心，而是保持冷静，等待想法成长，对吗？
en: We shouldn't worry but keep calm and wait for ideas to grow, right?

zh: 第三阶段被称为“评估”。
en: The third stage is called "evaluation".

zh: 一旦有一些想法出现，不要立即接受或行动，而是先仔细思考。
en: Once some ideas come up, don't accept or act immediately, but think carefully first.

zh: 问自己一些问题，比如：它真的有效吗？它是如何工作的？
en: Ask yourself questions like: Does it really work? How does it work?

zh: 然后，如果这个想法是合理的，最后一个阶段就是“阐述”。
en: Then if the idea is reasonable, the last stage is "elaboration".

zh: 恐怕我不明白“阐述”是什么意思。
en: I'm afraid I don't understand what "elaboration" means.

zh: 这意味着你要花很多很多时间来写出你的文章或制定你的商业计划。
en: This means that you spend hours and hours to write out your articles or make your business plans.

zh: 这就是整个创造过程。
en: That is the whole creative process.

zh: 感谢分享这些关于“顿悟时刻”的有趣观点！
en: Thanks for sharing those interesting views about "aha moments"!
